races.race_name: racingtv.com EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bella Lucia
runners.spotlight: Promising third when a well-supported 9-4 shot (from 9-2) on her debut at Beverley earlier this month (5f, good to soft); should improve on that, particularly now stepped up to 6f; one to consider.
runners.comment: Well-backed third on Beverley debut (5f); step up in trip should suit; one to consider

runners.name: Cheeky Chesca
runners.spotlight: Improved on her debut fifth here (5f, good to firm) when 2l fifth at Ripon last time (6f, good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Improved on her debut run here when 2l fifth at Ripon last time; each-way chance

runners.name: Time Glory
runners.spotlight: Home-bred filly by Cotai Glory; unraced dam is by Sea The Stars; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: By Cotai Glory; unraced dam is by Sea The Stars; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: One Shot Kodi
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros foal; by Kodi Bear; first foal of a 1m winner; stable's juveniles going well.
runners.comment: 16,000euros foal; by Kodi Bear; first foal of a 1m winner; stable's juveniles going well

runners.name: Liveadream
runners.spotlight: Ardad filly who cost £20,000 at the Goffs breeze-up in April having been bought for £10,000 as a yearling; first foal of an unraced mare by Havana Gold; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: £20,000 breeze-up buy; by Ardad out of unraced mare by Havana Gold; check the betting
runners.owner: Nick Bradley Racing 13 & P J Makin

runners.name: Nour
runners.spotlight: Filly by Dandy Man who cost 52,000gns as a yearling; half-sister to two winners (7f-1m6f); has a Super Sprint entry but likely to need further in time; worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: 52,000gns yearling; by Dandy Man; likely to need further in time; worth a market check

runners.name: Khazamh
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling who was bought for 20,000gns at the Newmarket Guineas breeze-up; half-sister to four winners out of a mare who won the 5f Listed Marygate Stakes at York; likely sort.
runners.comment: 20,000gns breeze-up buy; half-sister to 4 winners out of a Listed 5f winner; likely sort

runners.name: Priscilla's Hope
runners.spotlight: Beaten 7l when third on Yarmouth debut (6f, good to firm) and was behind Freedom Flame when last of six at Wolverhampton last time (6f); bit to find.
runners.comment: Behind Freedom Flame at Wolverhampton so probably best watched this time

runners.name: Freedom Flame
runners.spotlight: £40,000 yearling; by Kodi Bear out useful sprinting juvenile; beaten 1.5l when fourth of six in a Wolverhampton maiden on debut (6f; 7-4 joint favourite; second a good second on turf since); Super Sprint entry; interesting.
runners.comment: £40,000 yearling by Kodi Bear; close fourth on AW debut; improvement likely; interesting

races.race_name: Watch Irish Racing On Racing TV Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cape Fear
runners.spotlight: Beaten four times and more consistent than progressive; however, he's got the best form of these and looks sure to have a major say.
runners.comment: He's got the best form of these and looks sure to have a major say

runners.name: Hoseki
runners.spotlight: Her first run when third at Newbury (1m, soft) was better than the second at Newcastle; has potential over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Her first run when third at Newbury (1m, soft) was better than the second at Newcastle

runners.name: Talitha Rouge
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; closely related to 1m4f winner Bay Colony (RPR 85), half-sister to winners You Send Me (Group-placed 7f; 102) and Gerry's Way (1m; 71); dam 1m AW winner (73), half-sister to disqualified 1,000 Guineas winner Jacqueline Quest; nicely bred but she's the only runner lacking experience.
runners.comment: Nicely bred but she's the only runner lacking experience

runners.name: Champion Lawman
runners.spotlight: Learning on the job when beaten under 4l at Lingfield (1m) and interesting he was made favourite that day; should improve.
runners.comment: Learning on the job when beaten under 4l at Lingfield (1m); made favourite that day

runners.name: Poppeye
runners.spotlight: Not overly appealing even on his best run (debut over 1m4f) and this 4yo has to concede weight all round.
runners.comment: Limited appeal on what he's shown and he has to concede weight all round

runners.name: Matilda Bea
runners.spotlight: At 66-1 she was beaten a long way at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm); no appeal.
runners.comment: At 66-1 she was beaten a long way at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm); no appeal

races.race_name: Celebrate The Life Of Joe Newton Handicap

runners.name: Gaelic Approach
runners.spotlight: Record in small-field handicaps reads 212 and he finds himself in another one; may again enjoy the run of the race in front and has a good chance of seeing it out.
runners.comment: May again enjoy the run of the race in front and has a good chance of seeing it out
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin

runners.name: Wicksey
runners.spotlight: 3-3 in AW handicaps but record on turf reads 009; made all on his penultimate start and could be interesting if similar tactics are employed, but his suitability to grass remains an issue.
runners.comment: 3-3 in AW handicaps but record on turf reads 009; suitability to grass remains an issue

runners.name: Fairydale
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in six starts and unraced beyond 1m2f; usually held up in her races but not sure if this will pan out ideally for her; blinkers return.
runners.comment: Unraced beyond 1m2f; usually held up but not sure if this will pan out ideally for her

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap

runners.name: Nyman
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; not a regular winner, but ran a sound race when 2.5l fourth over C&D earlier this month (good); only a pound higher than when winning at Newmarket last autumn (7f, good to soft); place chance.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; not a regular winner; sound C&D 4th two runs back; on a fair mark; place chance

runners.name: Desert Shadow
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; two sound runs over 1m2f after being gelded last August (relegated after passing post in front on second occasion); disappointing at 1m4f this season, but drop to 1m not sure to suit; visor now tried.
runners.comment: 0-9; two sound runs after being gelded last August (1m2f); down in trip with visor tried

runners.name: Classic Encounter
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, over C&D in April (good) and off 1lb lower over 1m1f at York in June (good to firm; beat Cosmos Raj by a nose); not disgraced in two competitive handicaps this season and is the one to beat now down in grade.
runners.comment: C&D winner last term; fair runs in 2 competitive h'caps this season; chance down in grade

runners.name: Eligible
runners.spotlight: Veteran who won over 7f at Southwell in January but has been rather below his best since; equally effective on turf and has won over 1m on fast ground; others look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Veteran; won 7f AW h'cap in January, but below his best since; others better handicapped

runners.name: Cosmos Raj
runners.spotlight: Closely-matched with Classic Encounter on their form at York last June (1m1f, good to firm); bit disappointing since Ripon win in July (1m, good to soft), although excuses for poor reappearance run (blindfold removed late).
runners.comment: Closely matched with Classic Encounter on 2025 York form, but not at best last few runs.

runners.name: Andesite
runners.spotlight: Useful for Karl Burke in his younger days, although only win was a York novice on debut (6f, good); best run for Roger Fell when fifth over 7f at Thirsk last time, when shaping as though he might stay 1m (can pull hard); tongue-tie now tried.
runners.comment: Useful for Karl Burke, but only won once (6f); shaped as though 1m would suit last time

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap

runners.name: Territorial Star
runners.spotlight: Placed on four of her five starts, all over 6f, for Ed Bethell last season; only disappointing run was over 7f when she pulled too hard; sold for 6,000gns in September and not disgraced for new connections this time round (both at 6f); stamina to prove back up to 7f.
runners.comment: Fair form over 6f for Ed Bethell last season but her only disappointing run was at 7f

runners.name: Resdev Time
runners.spotlight: Fairly exposed now; best run was when third of eight in a Beverley novice last September (7.5f, soft); fair form in two 1m handicaps this time; may be suited by this drop in trip with headgear now tried.
runners.comment: Best run was over 7.5f in 2025; fair over 1m this term; drop to 7f could suit; visor tried

runners.name: Sweet Echo
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on her debut fourth in a Windsor maiden last August (6f, good to firm; slowly away), but has been disappointing since; may do better up to 7f for first time.
runners.comment: Good fourth after slow start on debut; very disappointing since; step up to 7f could suit

runners.name: Long Shot
runners.spotlight: Two good runs here this year, finishing third over 6f on reappearance (good to firm; form sound) and second when stepped up to 7f here last week (good); blinkers replace cheekpieces; chance.
runners.comment: Two good runs here this term (second over 7f here last week); new headgear tried; chance

runners.name: Positive Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Maiden who is making her handicap debut; fair fourth in a Musselburgh maiden last November (7f, soft) but better form on the AW; first run on fast ground.
runners.comment: Maiden; makes handicap debut; best efforts have been on AW; first run on fast ground

runners.name: Stromness
runners.spotlight: Fair effort when close fifth in a Newcastle novice on debut in January (6f); hasn't progressed (tried over 5f on reappearance) and cheekpieces now tried.
runners.comment: Fair debut run at Newcastle in January; hasn't progressed and now tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: Kameko Fever
runners.spotlight: Best run so far when neck second at Newcastle in March (1m); hasn't fared quite so well in two turf handicaps since (both over 1m) and now drops down to 7f with cheekpieces on for first time; interesting.
runners.comment: Good 2nd on AW in March; fair run over 1m latest; drops in trip with headgear on; chance
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: A Boy Named Mary
runners.spotlight: Only modest form so far; slowly away when well beaten over 1m here on reappearance; may do better.
runners.comment: Modest form so far; slowly away when well beaten here on reappearance; may do better

runners.name: Menwirr
runners.spotlight: Makes handicap/turf debut after three runs in 6f/7f AW novices, best run when fifth of seven over 7f at Lingfield in March; soundly beaten last time and cheekpieces now tried; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Makes handicap/turf debut after 3 runs on AW, best run when fifth over 7f; headgear tried

runners.name: Willowinghurn
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, a 6f maiden here in May (good to firm) and a 7f Musselburgh nursery in October (soft); disappointing so far this season.
runners.comment: Two wins in 2025, the first a 6f maiden here in May; disappointing in two runs this season

runners.name: Thornaby Annie
runners.spotlight: Best run so far when close second at Doncaster last time (7f, good to firm; hood on first time), running on well after meeting trouble in running; that wasn't a strong race, but could go well again.
runners.comment: Best run so far when running-on second at Doncaster last time (not best of runs); chance

runners.name: Rousham
runners.spotlight: Strong-puller who has had four runs in 6f/7f maiden/novices; well beaten second at Ripon last time (6f, good) but winner has trotted up in a handicap since; stable in decent form and could go well on handicap debut back at 7f if he settles better.
runners.comment: Strong-puller; unexposed; fair second to useful sort in 6f maiden last time; could go well

runners.name: Hunterian
runners.spotlight: Unexposed; makes his turf debut having finished well beaten in four runs at around a mile on the AW, including on handicap debut at Newcastle last time (February); may fare better down in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed; makes turf debut down in trip after four runs on AW; has a bit to prove

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV In Stunning HD Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Lady Bouquet
runners.spotlight: Still off the same mark as when successful at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in December; three-time winner on turf and ran well to finish third of 11 on her return from a break at Catterick (5f, good) 15 days ago; not the most consistent, but her best form would make her a big player.
runners.comment: Still off last winning mark; not very consistent but best form would make her a big player

runners.name: Doralee
runners.spotlight: 1-19, the win coming over C&D (good to firm) from 3lb lower last August; has a bit to find with Kyber Crystal on last month's course meeting, though.
runners.comment: 1-19, the win coming over C&D; has a bit to find with Kyber Crystal on April running here

runners.name: Ninety Nine
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come on ground on the soft side of good, but she does handle good to firm; back off her last winning mark, but she finished over 20l behind Lady Bouquet on her Catterick reappearance 15 days ago; first-time blinkers replace the usual cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Over 20l behind Lady Bouquet on her Catterick reappearance 15 days ago; blinkers on

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: 1-23 and her very best form has come on the AW, but she was only beaten just over 2l into fourth of 14 from 3lb higher at Doncaster (5f, good to firm) on her penultimate outing; possibilities if back to that sort of form.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than when fourth at Doncaster on penultimate start; possibilities on that form

runners.name: Jeany May
runners.spotlight: 1-20 and although 10lb below her sole winning mark, is hard to recommend having not beaten many in three starts since returning last month; visor will need to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy having not beaten many in three starts since returning last month; visor on

runners.name: Jamie's Choice
runners.spotlight: Both wins came in 0-50 classified events in the summer of 2024, including one over C&D; not much to shout about over the past year, though, despite a declining mark; needs the visor to help stop the slide.
runners.comment: Not much to shout about over the past year despite a declining mark; visor on

runners.name: Crocus Time
runners.spotlight: May have needed her Thirsk reappearance ten days ago, but just 1-28 and this is just her second attempt at 5f; others preferred.
runners.comment: Just 1-28 and this is just her second attempt at 5f; others preferred

runners.name: Aurora's Doublesix
runners.spotlight: Hasn't finished within 10l of the winner in 13 starts and was behind two of these when a well-beaten last of 11 at Catterick 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Hasn't finished within 10l of the winner in 13 starts

runners.name: Kyber Crystal
runners.spotlight: Had excuses for her Hamilton performance 11 days ago (anticipated the start and hit the gate) having previously made a promising reappearance when third of nine over this C&D (good to firm); 1lb below her last winning mark and very much of interest back in a 0-55.
runners.comment: 1lb below her last winning mark and very much of interest back in a 0-55

runners.name: Furrst Lady
runners.spotlight: Refused to race on debut and a well-beaten last in all three starts over 6f-1m since; makes no appeal on drop in trip/handicap debut.
runners.comment: A well-beaten last in all three completed starts; makes no appeal

races.race_name: Watch Race Replays On Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Mister Sky Blue
runners.spotlight: Entitled to win races off his current mark and kept on well for second (behind One Of Our Own) here 19 days ago after a break; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Kept on well for second here 19 days ago after a break; thereabouts

runners.name: Andalprofit
runners.spotlight: Last win was over C&D off this mark in November but he can be hard to predict and should have run better back in a 0-60 last time.
runners.comment: Can be hard to predict and should have run better back in a 0-60 last time

runners.name: Dream Deal
runners.spotlight: Had 12 races for current yard and won two of them; never threatened to add another when beaten 16l only last week; that was after a break.
runners.comment: Three wins but never threatened to add another when beaten 16l only last week

runners.name: Fircombe Hall
runners.spotlight: Has been running well on the AW but that's more his thing, the scene of nine of his ten wins; could still have a part to play.
runners.comment: AW is the scene of nine of his ten wins but he could still have a part to play

runners.name: Bayraat
runners.spotlight: Only a length away over C&D on his return from 222 days away but no certainty to follow one good run with another, and he finds winning difficult (1-31).
runners.comment: Second on his return but no certainty to follow one good run with another

runners.name: Golden Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f at Catterick in April (fine over 6f) but subsequent defeats underline his inconsistency.
runners.comment: Won over 5f at Catterick in April but subsequent defeats underline his inconsistency

runners.name: Evoluir
runners.spotlight: Just two wins in 34 starts and he hasn't been in great form on the AW, which usually brings out the best in him.
runners.comment: Just two wins in 34 starts and he hasn't been in great form on the AW

runners.name: Hyrcanian
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden who didn't run well enough at Catterick after her break to warrant a second look.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden and didn't run well enough at Catterick after her break

runners.name: Mart
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner and there have been some good efforts in his losing run of 16, though not of late; others appeal more.
runners.comment: There have been some good efforts in his losing run of 16, though not of late

runners.name: One Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: Beat Mister Sky Blue by 2l to win over C&D this month but was comfortably held off this 6lb higher mark last week; consistent.
runners.comment: Recent C&D winner; perhaps on a high mark but at least she's consistent

runners.name: Let's Go Hugo
runners.spotlight: All wins at 5f and he was only tenth over C&D recently, finishing behind One Of Our Own and Mister Sky Blue.
runners.comment: All wins at 5f and he was only tenth over C&D recently, finishing behind two of these

runners.name: Soul Seeker
runners.spotlight: Regressive 9yo who hasn't been offering much for a while now.
runners.comment: Regressive 9yo who hasn't been offering much for a while now

races.race_name: British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Victory Gold
runners.spotlight: Strong in the market at 5-2 when runner-up on debut at Ascot (6f, good) this month, having made the running; the winner has followed up and he sets a useful standard for the rest to aim at.
runners.comment: 5-2 when runner-up on debut at Ascot; winner has followed up; sets a useful standard

runners.name: High Calibre
runners.spotlight: 82,000gns foal, 42,000gns yearling; first foal; dam German 1m 2yo winner, half-sister to winners Grocer Jack (German/Italian 1m2f Group 3) and Ghislaine (German 7f Listed), out of German 1m3f Group 3 winner; this Lucky Vega newcomer is one to be interested in if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: 42,000gns yearling; first foal; dam German 1m 2yo winner; betting could be informative

runners.name: Savage Mariner
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros foal, 80,000euros yearling; sixth foal; Blackbeard half-brother to three winners notably Eternal Hope (1m1f-1m4f including US turf Grade 2); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (79), half-sister to 7f-1m2f Group 1 winner Rio De La Plata; trainer's 2yos have been going very well; chance on debut.
runners.comment: 80,000euros yearling; half-brother to three winners; yard's 2yos have been going very well

runners.name: Royal Message
runners.spotlight: Second foal; No Nay Never half-sister to 7f AW winner Champion Again Ole (RPR 86); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/Group 2), from talented family; takes on males and she's likely to improve for this debut run.
runners.comment: Dam 6f winner (including 2yo/Group 2); takes on males and likely to improve for this debut
runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber & W S Farish

runners.name: Captaincy
runners.spotlight: 42,000euros foal, 100,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 5f winner (RPR 77), closely related to 7f 2yo winner Charged Up, half-sister to 9.4f/1m2f winner Glorious Caesar, out of 7f 2yo Listed winner; trainer has 28% strike-rate with 2yos here in last five years; this Havana Grey newcomer is respected.
runners.comment: Debut; 100,000gns yearling; trainer has 28% strike-rate with 2yos here in last five years

runners.name: Rattenbury
runners.spotlight: 62,000euros foal, 75,000euros yearling; fifth foal; Blackbeard half-brother to 6f AW winner Dyed In The Wool (RPR 74); dam 5f 2yo winner (76), half-sister to four winners out of US 6f stakes winner; not ruled out on debut but he may well improve for the outing.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 6f AW winner Dyed In The Wool (RPR 74); may improve for this debut outing

runners.name: Libertango
runners.spotlight: 130,000euros yearling, 400,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; by No Nay Never; first foal; dam unplaced 6f/1m 2yo, sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Aktabantay, half-sister to winners Baydar (smart 1m-1m2f) and Reina Madre (7f Group 3), out of 1m2f Listed winner; takes on males on debut but could still have a big say.
runners.comment: 400,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; takes on males on debut but could still have a big say

runners.name: Miami To Ibiza
runners.spotlight: £30,000 yearling, 240,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; tenth foal; half-brother to four winners notably Bowland Park (7f-1m2f including 2yo; RPR 93); dam 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; last month's purchase price catches the eye and he could play a leading role on debut.
runners.comment: Went for 240,000gns at the breeze-ups last month and could play a leading role on debut

runners.name: Shoof
runners.spotlight: £36,000 yearling, £90,000 2yo breeze-ups; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to three winners including Legionario (1m Listed); trainer has already unearthed some useful 2yos and this Starman newcomer could be a contender.
runners.comment: Trainer has already unearthed some useful 2yos; this Starman newcomer could be a contender

runners.name: Valdoro
runners.spotlight: 130,000euros yearling; sixth foal; No Nay Never brother to winners Frescobaldi (Listed-placed 7f 2yo; RPR 94) and How Lovely (5f/6f including 2yo; 82), half-brother to US 5.5f turf 2yo winner Kodikova; dam placed 7f-8.5f (inc 2yo Listed); could give a good account on debut.
runners.comment: 130,000euros yearling; useful pedigree and could give a good account on debut

runners.name: Lion O
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling who is a half-brother to six winners but he needs to produce considerably more than he's managed on his first two starts.
runners.comment: Half-brother to six winners but has to produce considerably more than he's managed so far

races.race_name: Kube - Leicester's Premier Exhibition Venue Selling Stakes

runners.name: Showmethebubbles
runners.spotlight: Some late headway from the rear when eighth of 11 on last month's debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft); backward step there (5f, good to soft) since but has had a wind op subsequently and her initial effort hinted that this sort of trip may suit; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Minor late headway over 5f on debut; wind op since lesser run last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Jazzy Bay
runners.spotlight: Nibbled into 11-1 and beaten only about 3l when sixth of 12 on debut at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) last Thursday; that form makes him a leading player and he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when sixth of 12 at Chepstow and that form makes him a leading player

runners.name: Parading
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when keeping on for fifth of 11, beaten just under 3l, at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) a fortnight ago on his second start and that performance can be upgraded as he raced wide; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Close fifth on second start (6f, AW) and that performance can be upgraded as he raced wide

runners.name: Bluestone Lady
runners.spotlight: Second of six at Southwell (5f, AW) on May 7 but the form is modest and she made it 0-4 when sixth of 15 at Redcar (6f, good) last Monday; she may be vulnerable once more.
runners.comment: Made it 0-4 when sixth of 15 at Redcar and she may be vulnerable once more

runners.name: Abrahamsen
runners.spotlight: 7,000euros yearling; by Kodi Bear; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to some talented winners; makes debut in a seller but the standard is just a modest one and his trainer's 2yos are going well; could have a big part to play, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Makes debut in seller but standard is just a modest one and his yard's 2yos are going well

runners.name: Village Hall
runners.spotlight: Open to improvement on this second start but he was beaten a long way at 22-1 at Pontefract (5f, good) last month and needs to be an entirely different proposition today.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way at 22-1 on debut (5f, good) & needs to be entirely different proposition

races.race_name: British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Crownright
runners.spotlight: Third at Kempton (1m, AW) last November on sole 2yo start and again showed promise when runner-up at Ascot (1m, good to firm) this month, when failing to settle in a first-time hood (left off today); that latest form has been boosted by the winner and it's likely she'll continue to progress.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Ascot on second start and that form has been boosted by the winner

runners.name: Harlequin Breeze
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and it's possible others here have greater potential but she again showed useful form when fourth on handicap debut at Haydock (1m, good) this month; may well be in the mix.
runners.comment: 0-5 but has shown some useful form and she may well be in the mix

runners.name: Caim
runners.spotlight: Comfortable win on debut at Beverley (7.4f, soft) last September and runner-up in Listed race at Newbury (7f, soft) the following month; leading claims if transferring that form to today's better ground on her reappearance.
runners.comment: A win and a Listed second last autumn from her first two starts; leading form claims
runners.owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall

runners.name: Seet
runners.spotlight: Built upon last month's debut fifth at Newbury (7f, good) when winning with something to spare at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW); this well-bred filly could continue to progress for her top yard and is firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won with something to spare on second start and could continue to progress for top yard

runners.name: Molo Del Palazzo
runners.spotlight: In good hands (stablemate of Crownright) but she's raced twice and has plenty to find with all of today's opponents.
runners.comment: In good hands but she's raced twice and has plenty to find with all of today's opponents

runners.name: El Vamos
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling who was backed into 17-2 when an easy winner on debut at Ascot (1m, soft) last September; encounters different ground on this first run since but she could have lots more to offer and has to be respected.
runners.comment: 420,000gns yearling who won easily on debut last September; respected on first run since

races.race_name: Follow @leicesterraces On X Handicap

runners.name: Giles Glory
runners.spotlight: Unproven on turf and his form has been up and down since he won at Lingfield (7f, AW) in January, with a below-par run at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on last month's stable debut; however, he's not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Up and down since he won at Lingfield in January but he's not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Hatamoto
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward when sent off favourite and runner-up on handicap debut at Carlisle (1m, soft) last Monday; runs off the same mark today and he's a major player if transferring his improvement back to better ground.
runners.comment: Runner-up on handicap debut at Carlisle; major player if backing up that performance

runners.name: Elashgar
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in February and runner-up there (5f, AW) in March; not at the same level on turf debut at Nottingham (6f, good) last month, having been gelded during a 50-day break, and he's unproven at 7f, but is not written off.
runners.comment: Did well in AW h'caps earlier this year; soundly beaten on turf debut but not written off

runners.name: Woody Y Fernandez
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden but he was in pretty good heart this spring on his two starts for Stephen Hanlon (6f, AW/good) and shaped each time as though this return to 7f could be a positive move; possible player.
runners.comment: 0-12 but in pretty good heart over 6f this spring, shaping as though return to 7f may suit

runners.name: She's Crafty
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but has run well on two of her three starts this season, including over C&D and on latest start when 6l second to an improved winner at Lingfield (7f, good); she might not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-11 but a couple of good runs this season (including C&D) and she might not be far away

runners.name: Zoulette
runners.spotlight: Slowly away on recent handicap/turf debut at Windsor (6f, good) but made late headway into seventh of 12 and this return to 7f could prove ideal.
runners.comment: Late headway over 6f on recent handicap debut and this return to 7f could prove ideal

runners.name: Telling Time
runners.spotlight: He went close off 2lb higher at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in March and a case can be made on that form but he's been soundly beaten back on turf on his last two starts; now 0-9 and he has to raise his game.
runners.comment: Went close on AW in March but soundly beaten on turf on both starts since and now 0-9

runners.name: Muvable
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Redcar this spring in first two handicaps (6f/1m, good to firm/good) and a transformation is needed; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Well beaten at Redcar this spring in first two handicaps; blinkers replace cheekpieces

runners.name: Snow Day
runners.spotlight: Goes handicapping off basement mark and with yard in good form, and she's one to watch in the betting, but she's been beaten a long way at massive odds on her first three starts (all 7f, AW).
runners.comment: Goes handicapping with yard in good form but beaten a long way on her first three starts

runners.name: Escape Magic
runners.spotlight: Achieved little across her three starts towards end of last year but she's from a respected Lambourn yard and goes handicapping off a basement mark; no surprise if she takes a big step forward, especially if well backed.
runners.comment: Has achieved little but goes handicapping off a basement mark; one to watch in the betting

runners.name: Girls Allowed
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on her three 2yo starts (7f/6f); makes handicap debut off a lowly mark but improvement is necessary.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten thus far; makes handicap debut off lowly mark but improvement is necessary

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Today Handicap

runners.name: Happy Chandler
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on reappearance at Bath (1m, good) last month then won over C&D (good to firm) before a good second at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm); arrives at the top of his game and he's a strong contender.
runners.comment: He's been at the top of his game this spring and is a strong contender

runners.name: Myna
runners.spotlight: Finally got off the mark at Lingfield (1m2f, good) and has continued in good form in the main since, narrowly winning over this C&D (good to firm) last time; just 1lb higher today and he's high on the list.
runners.comment: Narrow win over C&D last time and is just 1lb higher today; he's high on the list

runners.name: Charencey
runners.spotlight: His losing run goes back to July 2024 but he's 4lb lower than when a respectable fourth over C&D (good) last September and could build on last month's fifth at Nottingham; not discounted; non-runner 5:18 Redcar yesterday.
runners.comment: Losing run goes back to July 2024 but on a reduced mark; non-runner 5:18 Redcar yesterday

runners.name: Beauty Generation
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts on last two starts, finishing third at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) then fourth of 14 over this C&D (good to firm); 0-11 on turf but has an each-way shout.
runners.comment: Respectable efforts on last two starts (C&D latest); 0-11 on turf but has each-way shout

runners.name: Study Up
runners.spotlight: 0-9 since debut win at Salisbury in July 2024 and untested at 1m2f, but he's dropped down the weights and was a very close fourth here (1m, good to firm) last time; could be in the mix if seeing out the trip.
runners.comment: Very close fourth over 1m here last time and could be in the mix if seeing out the trip

runners.name: Ottoman
runners.spotlight: He's on a handy mark on his form last June/July, when winning over C&D, and a market check is advised on his reappearance; however, he went off the boil last autumn and returns minus cheekpieces (worn when winning).
runners.comment: C&D winner; on a handy mark but went off the boil last autumn; returns minus cheekpieces

runners.name: San Francisco Bay
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on heavy ground at Nottingham last October when last seen but showed some good form last season, including when winning at Nottingham (1m2f, good), and he returns on a competitive mark; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent run but he returns on a competitive mark and is not ruled out

runners.name: Staffordshire
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at the 11th attempt at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in February, pulling clear with a next-time-out winner, and didn't get the chance to show what he could do back there in March (saddle slipped); probably acts on turf and he's in with a chance.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in February and saddle slipped there next time; he's in with a chance

runners.name: Cartwheel
runners.spotlight: She made a promising start to her career and has tumbled down the weights, but she's struggled to get competitive this season and a major turnaround is needed in a first-time tongue-tie and reapplied hood.
runners.comment: She's tumbled down the weights but has struggled to get competitive this season

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Frantic
runners.spotlight: Promise over C&D on 2yo debut; better form when second of ten at Nottingham last month (6f, good); open to further progress and her opening mark looks manageable; good chance.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two of her three runs, including C&D; opening mark fair; good chance

runners.name: Always Perfect
runners.spotlight: 8lb better off with Frantic for 2.75l on their Nottingham run last month; not in quite the same form at Southwell 19 days ago and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ties in with Frantic on Nottingham form but latest Southwell third not quite so good

runners.name: Filly Foden
runners.spotlight: Two 5f AW wins early in the year, the second of them by a wide margin at Lingfield; the handicapper has won the battle since and she was down the field returned to turf at Windsor three weeks ago; mark more realistic again now but she will need more to take this.
runners.comment: Handicapper in command after two AW wins this winter; below her best back on turf latest

runners.name: Anorah Unleashed
runners.spotlight: No real improvement for the switch to handicaps; a tongue-tie is now reached for and it needs to give her a lift.
runners.comment: No improvement for handicapping as yet; a tongue-tie is now reached for

runners.name: Polka Blue
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden but she has shown clear promise, including on her handicap debut last month; not quite so good back in a novice last time; return to sprinting needs to give her a lift.
runners.comment: 0-6 but she has shown promise, including on her handicap debut; now drops back in trip

runners.name: Nebbia
runners.spotlight: Unplaced on this month's handicap/seasonal debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) but she wasn't beaten far into fifth and she shaped as though the run would bring her on; capable of better.
runners.comment: Promising seasonal/handicap debut at Windsor this month; still has time to do better

runners.name: Yasalam
runners.spotlight: 2yo debut (7f, AW) was encouraging but she has been well short of that level in two runs this spring; not obviously well treated but Oisin Murphy booked for her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Needs to build on 2yo debut promise; Oisin Murphy booked for handicap debut; check betting

runners.name: Blindfold Games
runners.spotlight: Had some fair AW form over the winter but this looks a stronger race and she lacks the potential of some of her rivals.
runners.comment: Fair AW form over the winter but this looks tougher back on the grass

runners.name: Hewi
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in three runs last autumn (all 6f, two on AW and debut on good ground); should have more to come this year but she'll need it to defy this mark.
runners.comment: Three 2yo runs offered hope of better but she doesn't look to have been let in lightly

races.race_name: Next Meeting Monday 8th June Handicap

runners.name: Alice De Clare
runners.spotlight: Improving filly who made short work of her six rivals at Yarmouth last month (1m2f, good to firm); hit with a 9lb rise but it might not be enough to prevent the follow up.
runners.comment: Improving filly; won decisively at Yarmouth last month; 9lb rise may not prevent follow up

runners.name: Zipwire
runners.spotlight: He has improved for the addition of cheekpieces but not sure there is much mileage in his mark and that isn't the case with a few of his rivals.
runners.comment: Improved for cheekpieces but looks exposed and others have more pressing claims
runners.owner: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore

runners.name: Fille Imbassee
runners.spotlight: 1m AW win for Marco Botti in January; promising start for new stable in March but backward steps twice since and she was well beaten on her turf debut last month; others are stronger.
runners.comment: 1m AW win for M Botti; disappointing on last two runs, markedly so on turf debut latest

runners.name: Zooter
runners.spotlight: Promise in novice/maiden company but he has finished out of the places in both handicaps (5-4f for nursery debut); cheekpiece experiment quickly aborted; up to 1m2f for the first time with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Unplaced in both handicaps; steps up in trip with a point to prove

runners.name: Pentonville
runners.spotlight: Clear signs of ability in three novice runs at up to 1m; disappointing if this 295,000gns yearling couldn't do better now handicapping at a low level; up in trip with cheekpieces fitted; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Up in trip and tried in headgear for handicap debut; bred to be much better than this
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Download The Racecourse App Raceday Ready Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Bownder
runners.spotlight: For one who has been a model of consistency for much of his career it is slightly disconcerting that he has been well below form in three of his last four starts; the lesser runs have been in Class 5 events and his standout recent effort came when faced with Class 6 opposition, missing out by just a head at Lingfield in February (1m2f, AW); has run well over C&D in the past and a revival could be forthcoming back down in grade.
runners.comment: Disappointing in 3 of last 4 runs but returning to Class 6 will help; revival possible

runners.name: Nammos
runners.spotlight: 20-1 for her stable debut here two weeks ago but she kept on well to see off nine rivals (1m, firm); still has plenty of handicapping scope and she could be the answer.
runners.comment: Winning stable debut over C&D 2 weeks ago (firm); up 3lb but still has handicapping scope

runners.name: Danehill Star
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins over further this year; fourth over C&D on Friday, finishing 1.25l behind the reopposing Rating; apprentice's claim useful today and he shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this year; 4th over C&D on Friday when 1.25l behind the reopposing Rating

runners.name: The Dark Baron
runners.spotlight: Course winner (1m2f, good to soft) last September and he has made good progress since, adding two AW successes (9.4f/7f) to his tally; close third over C&D last month (good ground) but not in the same form at Kempton three weeks ago; can't ignore if as effective on today's faster ground.
runners.comment: Progressive until a slight blip last time; effective at Bath; such lively ground a query

runners.name: Rating
runners.spotlight: 22nd time lucky when landing a 9.4f handicap at Wolverhampton in December; three good runs at Bath this spring, most recently a close third over C&D on Friday; another big run on the cards.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but she arrives in form and conditions are fine; contender

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands C And D) (EBF Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Sable Island
runners.spotlight: Green and shaped quite nicely when third of eight on her Southwell debut four weeks ago; that form isn't working out though and she will need a good step forward to take this.
runners.comment: Promise amidst inexperience on debut four weeks ago (5f, AW); can do better but needs to

runners.name: Arabica Queen
runners.spotlight: Showed speed before weakening into fourth of the six runners on her Windsor debut three weeks ago (6f, good to firm); should last longer today and she is capable of better.
runners.comment: Weakened in final furlong on Windsor debut (6f, good to firm) this month; can do better

runners.name: Cydney Sweenie
runners.spotlight: Pulled hard when third at Wolverhampton two weeks ago (6f; 11-8 favourite) but she was still keeping on at the finish; the addition of a hood can help but she will need to up her game a notch or two.
runners.comment: Promise in both starts, pulling hard when 3rd on AW two weeks ago; now hooded; needs more

runners.name: Nasserein
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling by Bungle Inthejungle; first foal of an unraced half-sister to winners Shamlahar (1m) and Imperial Dawn (Norwegian 4.5f-1m); stable's 2yo newcomers always respected but others appear to have stronger paper claims.
runners.comment: 22,000gns yearling; unraced dam a half-sister to two winners; stable's 2yos going well

runners.name: Snow On The Beach
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns foal, 30,000gns breeze-up 2yo; by Mohaather out of a 5f 2yo winner (RPR 73), herself a half-sister to 7f Group 2 winner Tariq; both the stable's 2yo winners this year were successful at Bath, one on debut; market instructive.
runners.comment: 30,000gns breeze-up 2yo; dam a well-related 5f winner; stable 2-5 with 2yos here in 2026

runners.name: Eabha
runners.spotlight: By Space Blues; first foal of an unraced half-sister to 2m/2m1f hurdle winner Ebasari, out of Listed-placed 9.4f winning half-sister to 12.5f Group 2 winner Ebiyza, from thee family of Gold Cup winners Estimate and Enzeli; stable have winning newcomers but this filly may need longer trips before she is of major interest.
runners.comment: By Space Blues out of a mare from a stamina-laden family; may need more of a test

runners.name: Ennerdale Water
runners.spotlight: By A'Ali; third foal; half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Bassenthwaite (including AW; RPR 93); dam 6f AW winner (71) from a good sprinting family; hooded for debut; market instructive.
runners.comment: Half-sister to useful 2yo winner; dam 6f winner from good family; hood on debut; respected

runners.name: Acclamation Star
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to 9.4f AW winner Ballarat Bertie (RPR 50); dam Italian 7.5f/1m winner (including 2yo/Listed; 95); stable's 2yos tend to come on for their initial experience.
runners.comment: 10,000gns half-sister to 9.4f AW winner Ballarat Bertie; yard's newcomers better for a run

runners.name: Martha Brown
runners.spotlight: Her second run (C&D, firm) was better than her debut but she will need much more if she is to come out on top this time.
runners.comment: Third over C&D 13 days ago but that represents just modest forrm; opposable again

runners.name: Agnes Hathaway
runners.spotlight: Too green to do herself justice when eighth of 11 (Cydney Sweenie third) at Wolverhampton two weeks ago (6f, AW); should do better in time but others may have her measure today.
runners.comment: Green when eighth (Cydney Sweenie third) at Wolverhampton on debut; open to improvement
runners.owner: D Kilburn, C Wright, F Mahony & Partner

runners.name: Happy Humpo
runners.spotlight: 12-1 and always in rear when last of 11 at Beverley six weeks ago (5f, good to soft); should do better at some point.
runners.comment: 12-1 and always in rear on last month's Beverley debut; a big step forward is required

runners.name: Yahaira
runners.spotlight: Down the field in a 5f maiden here on debut before facing a tough task in the Lily Agnes last time; today's extra yardage needs to spark significant progress.
runners.comment: Down the field in two 5f runs this spring; down in grade but others have achieved more

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Phoenix Moon
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner but 0-9 on turf; ran her best race on grass when second at Windsor 19 days ago (6f, good to firm) but there were no excuses and this is a higher grade.
runners.comment: Four AW wins but 0-9 on turf; latest second came in a Class 6; vulnerable for the win

runners.name: Tuscan Point
runners.spotlight: Back on the up for Adam Kirby this spring, winning at Southwell (6f, AW) and Lingfield (5f, AW) as well as three second-place finishes; 0-16 on turf but effective on fast ground and this intermediate trip could be ideal.
runners.comment: Back on the up for new yard, winning twice on AW; 0-16 on turf but still a major player

runners.name: Strike
runners.spotlight: Won three times in 2024 but unable to add to his haul since; retains ability but perhaps vulnerable for win purposes once more.
runners.comment: Losing run stands at 16; retains ability but others appeal more for the win
runners.owner: S Emmet And Miss R Emmet

runners.name: Early Release
runners.spotlight: His last two wins have come when making all in Class 6 handicaps at Kempton over 6f; yet to win on turf but he has gone well on fast ground in the past; Rossa Ryan booked and he should give it a good go from the front.
runners.comment: Still to win on turf but Rossa Ryan is booked & he should give it a good go from the front

runners.name: Obsidian Dream
runners.spotlight: C&D winner as a 2yo; stood little racing since 2024 but there were more promising signs at Lingfield (7f, AW) when last seen in January; others look stronger.
runners.comment: C&D win as a 2yo; stood little racing since; back from another break with a point to prove

races.race_name: Tips For Every Race At raceday-ready.com Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Aces Wild
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who broke his turf duck at Chester last July off a 2lb lower mark (5f, good); two respectable efforts here this spring and Finley Marsh replaces an apprentice today; a big run is on the cards.
runners.comment: On a feasible mark and should find this being run to suit;a big run is on the cards

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; 0-10 on turf but placed at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and here (5.7f, firm) on his last two starts; cheekpieces return after one run in blinkers.
runners.comment: Front-runner; 0-10 on turf but arrives on the back of two placed efforts; considered
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth, Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: C&D win on May 4; came up shy under a penalty at Windsor one week later (race won by Correspondence) but she can race off 2lb lower today; should run her race but it won't be easy trying to dominate in this field.
runners.comment: C&D win this month; 5th behind Correspondence one week later; other pace influences here

runners.name: Diomed Spirit
runners.spotlight: Third, beaten 1.25l by Over Spiced, in a C&D handicap three weeks ago; that represented his best turf result but this race will demand an even bigger effort.
runners.comment: Third to Over Spiced here three weeks ago represented his best turf result; more needed

runners.name: Correspondence
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the tenth attempt (first in cheekpieces) when keeping on strongly to win at Windsor 15 days ago (Danger Alert fourth and Over Spiced fifth); up 5lb but still unexposed in the context of this field.
runners.comment: Off the mark in new cheekpieces at Windsor 15 days ago (two of these in behind); contender

runners.name: Level Up
runners.spotlight: Running well in defeat in recent months, including over C&D; should remain competitive but this is a step back up in class.
runners.comment: Knocking at the door of late, including over C&D; up in class but should run his race

runners.name: Danger Alert
runners.spotlight: Over three years since his last win; fourth to Correspondence on his reappearance but took a backward step at Windsor on Saturday; blinkers the headgear of choice today; others are stronger.
runners.comment: On long losing run and he was well short of his best at Windsor on Saturday

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com 'Confined' Handicap (Not Won Since 2025 Flat Turf Season)

runners.name: Baynoona
runners.spotlight: 1m win here last September off a 2lb lower mark; two respectable runs here last month and stepping back up in trip can help; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Course winner (1m); two fair efforts here last month but needs more for the extra distance
runners.owner: Miss Jane Edgar & D V Gardner

runners.name: Annexation
runners.spotlight: Won twice on turf in the autumn of 2025, including C&D; well handicapped on that form and his last two efforts have been respectable; should go well with Rossa Ryan up for the first time.
runners.comment: C&D winner; on a handy mark and Rossa Ryan up for the first time; should go well

runners.name: Show Biz Kid
runners.spotlight: 5lb lower than for last April's Yarmouth win (1m2f, good); solid fourth at Windsor two weeks ago and now drops in class; high on the list.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark and he has run two solid races in defeat this year; solid contender

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: 1m win here (heavy) last October came off a 6lb lower mark; solid efforts for new trainer here on his last two runs (extended 1m3f) but he could help set this up for a finisher.
runners.comment: Course winner; fair efforts over further here on last two starts; others appeal more

runners.name: Rovinia
runners.spotlight: 2lb lower than for her AW success (7f) last October; little to celebrate this year and her stamina for 1m2f has to be taken on trust.
runners.comment: 7f AW win last October came off 2lb higher; quiet this year and stamina a concern

runners.name: Racing Demon
runners.spotlight: Five wins at 1m, the latest two coming at this track in 2024; didn't run badly on his seasonal return here a fortnight ago (1m, firm); capable off this mark but the start is always a major concern where he is concerned.
runners.comment: Conditions should suit and feasibly weighted; the start is always a major concern for him

runners.name: Knightmare
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but there have been some good runs along the way, including here; others look to have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden; the odd run that makes him of interest but opposable on balance

runners.name: Dissident
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner in December but well short of that level over hurdles and in one Flat run this year; down in the weights but with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Hurdle win in December has been followed by lesser runs under both codes

runners.name: Jimmy Mark
runners.spotlight: Course winner (extended 1m3f) on last April's reappearance; ended that campaign with some lesser efforts but he returns on a feasible mark and can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Course winner who won after an absence here last April; feasible mark; not ruled out

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Station Bar
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has twice failed to back up March's promising Kempton fourth (1m); returning to 1m can help but vulnerable to progressive rivals.
runners.comment: 0-8; promising 4th on return in March (1m, AW) but he has failed to back it up twice since

runners.name: Legacy Rock
runners.spotlight: 2-2 since upped to a mile, winning a pair of Class 5 events at Windsor, the latest on Saturday in comfortable fashion; has a penalty to shoulder but he hasn't reached his ceiling just yet.
runners.comment: 2-2 over 1m and he won well on Saturday; big player despite his 6lb penalty

runners.name: Mohmentous
runners.spotlight: Not looked back since switched to turf, winning readily over C&D (firm) and following up at Redcar 19 days ago; that form is working out okay and a 5lb higher mark shouldn't prevent another prominent showing.
runners.comment: 2-2 on turf, winning well over C&D and at Redcar; still has more to offer

runners.name: Phantom Shadow
runners.spotlight: Ran his best race so far when a front-running second over C&D 13 days ago (clear of the third); runs off the same mark and he needs to raise his game again.
runners.comment: Second of five over C&D 13 days ago; runs off the same mark but needs improvement to win

races.race_name: Auray Assessors Fillies Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Urgent Whisper
runners.spotlight: 63-rated Flat maiden; showed improvement when switched to hurdling last summer, early in the juvenile campaign; placed on both Killarney starts, though beaten 10l on the second occasion; needs to find a bit extra.
runners.comment: Placed on both starts last, beaten 10l on the second occasion, needs to find a bit extra

runners.name: Cinating
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in three bumpers; has the advantage of that experience over stablemate My Elusive Mate; should go close with an adequate round of jumping
runners.comment: Has made the frame in three bumpers, should go close with an adequate round of jumping

runners.name: Witches Familiar
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to three winners; dam placed over hurdle, half-sister to brilliant staying hurdler Big Buck's; ran twice on the Flat in France last autumn; appeals as an interesting recruit for top connections.
runners.comment: Out of a half-sister to brilliant stayer Big Buck's, notable recruit for top connections

runners.name: My Elusive Mate
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam was a Dermot Weld-trained Monsun mare who stayed well and was suited by quick ground; lacks the experience of stablemate Cinating.
runners.comment: Dam stayed well on the Flat and was suited by quick ground; stablemate of Cinating.

runners.name: Tex Amare
runners.spotlight: Dam won an Italian Group 3 event and the Slovakian Oaks, out of a half-sister to Group 1 winner Tiger Hill; showed ability when second in a 1m2f maiden at Munich last October.
runners.comment: Showed ability when second in a 1m2f maiden at Munich last October, decent Flat pedigree

runners.name: Ismahane
runners.spotlight: Flat winner in Germany (1m1f, soft); runner-up in Italian St Leger (Group 3) before changing hands for E72,000; held a Triumph Hurdle entry at the time of her introduction at Thurles in January; betrayed inexperience and finished a well-beaten fourth; can improve.
runners.comment: Second in Italian St Leger (Group 3), betrayed inexperience on hurdling debut, can improve

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Down the field on hurdles debut at Killarney earlier this month; fair Flat run at a modest level last week; others preferred.
runners.comment: Down the field on hurdles debut at Killarney, fair Flat run at a modest level last week

runners.name: Green Me Granny
runners.spotlight: In rear in two bumpers; blinkers now instead of cheekpieces; transformation needed.
runners.comment: In rear in two bumpers, blinkers now instead of cheekpieces, transformation needed

runners.name: Ribadora
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in an academy hurdle last October; 12l behind Cinating in a recent Cork bumper.
runners.comment: Tailed off in an academy hurdle last October, 12l behind Cinating in a recent Cork bumper

runners.name: Dont Break My Word
runners.spotlight: Tailed off a long way from home on debut at Sligo; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Tailed off a long way from home on debut at Sligo, can be ruled out

runners.name: Turlough Abu
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds for four Flat maidens and a maiden hurdle and has run accordingly.
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds for four Flat maidens and a maiden hurdle and has run accordingly

races.race_name: KPMG Hurdle

runners.name: Immediate Effect
runners.spotlight: Dual AW Flat winner; made a winning hurdles debut at Punchestown last New Year's Eve; respectable run in a Grade 1 next time; below that standard at Naas; more like it when second to a Willie Mullins-trained outsider in a novice contest at the Punchestown festival; this ground should suit.
runners.comment: Has run in good company, pleasing effort when second at the Punchestown festival

runners.name: Kai Lung
runners.spotlight: Won over 7.5f on the Flat in France (soft); relegated subsequent high achiever Saratoga to third when landing a 4yo maiden hurdle at Naas in January (2m, heavy); major doubts raised by his last three runs; pulled up in the Triumph; beaten when unseating at Fairyhouse; dropped away in Punchestown Grade 1; tongue-tie now added to hood.
runners.comment: Naas win in January seemed promising, things have gone downhill since then

runners.name: Porcupine Bank
runners.spotlight: Jumping was untidy at times on debut at Tramore last month; owed his third placing to a faller at the last; stepped up from that when beating a short-priced runner for the Willie Mullins stable at Punchestown 13 days ago; helps to give Gordon Elliott a strong hand
runners.comment: Beat a short-priced runner from the Willie Mullins stable at Punchestown; definite chance

runners.name: Madness d'Elle
runners.spotlight: Placed on sole start for Daniela Mele at Auteuil last spring; beaten at short odds on Irish debut at Punchestown in January; made amends despite racing keenly at Gowran (2m, heavy); failed to make his presence felt in the Fred Winter; behind stablemate Kai Lung when tailed off at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Questions to answer after high-level failures, broadly the same profile as Kai Ling

runners.name: Munsif
runners.spotlight: Won three of his six races (at up to 1m4f) for Roger Varian and was useful; 0-5 over hurdles but bar one blip it's been a promising start; placed in three of his four starts over hurdles in Ireland, including a Naas event in which the top-class Saratoga was second; hung badly right when failing to justify support in a juvenile handicap hurdle at Ascot.
runners.comment: Placed in three of his four starts over hurdles in Ireland, disappointed in Ascot handicap

runners.name: In The Minus
runners.spotlight: Flat winner (7f) on good ground at Limerick last summer; has been error-prone over hurdles; best effort when winning at Galway last October; fit from a recent Flat outing at this venue; not suited by the race conditions.
runners.comment: Has been error-prone over hurdles, not suited by the race conditions.

races.race_name: Sheridan Electric Maiden Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Manoir De Mirande
runners.spotlight: 1m4f French bumper winner on very soft; placed in four of six maiden hurdles for this yard on soft ground; should be in the mix again if handling quicker conditions (not certain on breeding).
runners.comment: Placed in four of six maiden hurdles on soft, in the mix if handling quicker ground

runners.name: The Store Boy
runners.spotlight: In the frame all three starts in maiden hurdles on decent ground and ran well in a Leopardstown handicap on yielding in December; beaten 2.25l in a Kilbeggan maiden (16.5f, gd-yld) on latest on return from 99-day break; yard red-hot and likely to be thereabouts again with conditions to suit.
runners.comment: Placed in three maidens on nice ground, runner up at Kilbeggan after break latest, chance

runners.name: Killowen House
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to talented Chantry House; off the mark at fifth attempt in points at Quakerstown in April; promising run on hurdles debut at Cork when a 10.5l third over 3m (good); big drop in trip now; place shout.
runners.comment: Point winner, promise over 3m on hurdles debut, big drop in trip now, place shout

runners.name: Maxfort
runners.spotlight: Best effort over hurdles on debut at Cork in January when a 5l third of 17; not as good on heavy the last twice but could rebound returned to better ground.
runners.comment: Fine run on debut at Cork, struggled on heavy next twice, could rebound on better ground

runners.name: Press The Button
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner at Thurles in October (2m, gd-yld); soundly beaten in Listed bumper at Navan when last seen when testing ground may not have suited; may do better returned to a sounder surface on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Good ground bumper winner, struggled on heavy latest, ground more suitable now

runners.name: Eastwestconnection
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in sole point-to-point; tongue-tie goes on for Rules' debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Pulled up in sole point, tongue tie on for hurdles debut, best watched

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 1-16 in points; placed in a couple of hunter chases under rules; unlikely winner on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: 1-16 in points, placed in a couple of hunter chases, unlikely on hurdles bow; reserve

runners.name: Sunny Dude
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden 0-15 in that sphere and rated 36; hard to make a case for on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Flat maiden, rated 36, hard to make a case for on hurdles debut

runners.name: Mary's Up In Court
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in both points; tailed off in Sligo bumper latest; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Pulled up in both points, tailed off in Sligo bumper, safe to rule out; reserve

runners.name: Nothintodowithme
runners.spotlight: Mizzou gelding; unraced dam out of maiden half-sister to 2m5f chase winner Asking Questions; likely best watched on debut in tongue tie.
runners.comment: Mizzou gelding, dam unraced, tongue tied for debut, best watched

runners.name: Dar Toungi
runners.spotlight: Eight Flat wins when trained in France including at Listed level; nowhere near that form in three runs on the level for this yard; likely best watched on hurdles debut unless money comes.
runners.comment: No sign of smart French Flat form for this yard, watch on hurdles debut unless backed

runners.name: Capri Gear
runners.spotlight: Capri gelding; dam well beaten in bumper, half-sister to winners Red House Hill (bumper/11.3f Flat), All Clenched Up (bumper/2m hurdle) and Sand Fly (bumper/2m4f hurdle), out of bumper and useful 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner; market check advised.
runners.comment: Capri gelding, dam half-sister to hurdles winners, market check advised

runners.name: Evangelic
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat for Charles Hills; heavy defeat in Downpatrick maiden hurdle latest; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten on hurdling debut at Downpatrick, others preferred

runners.name: Mydreamtell
runners.spotlight: Telescope gelding; dam point winner, half-sister to winners Its Crucial (2m-3m) and Baldadash (2m5f) and to dam of high-class 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Andy Dufresne; yard had a recent bumper winner; check market.
runners.comment: Telescope gelding, dam point winner, yard had recent bumper winner, check market

runners.name: Extra Beat
runners.spotlight: Flat winner on soft ground for James Horton; pulled up when last seen on the AW at Lingfield in December; bought for 1,000gns in March; best watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Flat winner on soft in Britain, bought for 1,000gns in March, watch on hurdles debut

runners.name: Drumconnor Hill
runners.spotlight: Placed in one of two starts in point-to-points; 66-1 and tailed off on hurdles debut at Killarney; may need more time.
runners.comment: Tailed off on hurdles debut when 66-1 at Killarney, may need more time

runners.name: Finns First
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; huge odds and ran accordingly in maiden hurdles at Bellewstown and Sligo; one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Huge odds and well beaten in two maiden hurdles, one for handicaps

runners.name: Shire Star
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and never landed a blow on debut in a Killarney maiden hurdle; hard to fancy on that effort.
runners.comment: 66-1 and tailed off on debut at Killarney, hard to fancy on that evidence

races.race_name: Sheridan Electric Maiden Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Crystabel
runners.spotlight: 0-4 over hurdles and rated 97; best effort yet an 11l third of 16 at Sligo latest (2m2f, good) on return from a 200-day break; yard's runners always respected here and shorter trip will suit; place chance.
runners.comment: 0-4 over hurdles, rated 97, promising third at Sligo after break latest, place chance
runners.owner: M R D Fleming & G Bruce & J P Ryan & Miss G Ryan

runners.name: Sea Eagle
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat, 2-15 and rated 82; best effort from three starts over hurdles a 4.75l second of 15 at Downpatrick last September; still in contention when unseating at the last on return from a break at Tramore (2m, soft); in the mix.
runners.comment: Useful Flat performer, in contention when unseating at the last at Tramore latest, player

runners.name: Western Princess
runners.spotlight: With a top yard but yet to show winning potential and is a tricky ride; caught the eye of the stewards here last time when a 17l fifth; more needed to get her head in front.
runners.comment: Tricky ride, caught the eye of stewards here last time, more needed to get head in front

runners.name: Jerry From Kerry
runners.spotlight: Course bumper winner; rated 110 after three runs over hurdles, the best of which came on latest at Killarney when an 11l fourth; could go well.
runners.comment: Course bumper winner, promising run at Killarney latest, rated 110, could go well

runners.name: Marco Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Grade 3 bumper winner in France in July 2025 (1m4f, gd-sft); half-brother to very useful hurdler and chaser Hercule Du Seuil; of obvious interest on hurdles debut for top yard.
runners.comment: French Grade 3 bumper winner, half-brother to Hercule Du Seuil, of obvious interest

runners.name: Lady Felicia
runners.spotlight: By top jumps sire Jukebox Jury; dam useful 2m4f/2m5f hurdle winner, half-sister to three jumps winners; likely to need further than this in time.
runners.comment: Jukebox Jury mare, dam useful 2m4f/2m5f winner, may need further in time
runners.owner: Ms Margaret Nevin & Ms Alana Insole

runners.name: Saith Seren
runners.spotlight: 0-13 on the Flat and rated 48; not a bad effort on the level at Killarney latest but unlikely to make an impact on hurdles debut; watch.
runners.comment: 0-13 on the Flat and rated 48, unlikely to make an impact on hurdles debut, watch

runners.name: Humble Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; Berkshire mare; dam unraced half-sister to 1m6f Flat and smart 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Starchitect, out of 1m2f Listed winner; market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Berkshire mare, dam half-sister to smart Starchitect, market best guide; reserve

runners.name: Alentejo
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three Flat maidens for William Haggas in 2024; best watched on hurdles debut in first-time tongue-tie after huge layoff.
runners.comment: Modest Flat form when last seen in 2024, tongue tied, watch after long layoff

runners.name: Tullyegan Jet
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on debut in a Navan bumper; tailed off latest in Sligo mares' maiden hurdle; put a line through.
runners.comment: Pulled up on debut in Navan bumper, tailed off in Sligo maiden hurdle, put a line through

runners.name: Silken Thomas
runners.spotlight: Harzand gelding; E90,000 3yo; half-brother to bumper/2m hurdle winner La Note Verte; dam useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to French 2m2f Listed hurdle Eclipse De Cotte; appeals on paper; check the market.
runners.comment: E90,000 Harzand gelding, dam useful hurdles/chase winner, appeal on paper, check market

runners.name: Joe Cool
runners.spotlight: 0-4 on the Flat but went close in maidens at the Curragh (1m, heavy) and Bellewstown (11.5f, soft) the last twice; half-brother to a hurdles winner so should take to this game; good ground is a query, however.
runners.comment: Placed on soft/heavy last two starts on Flat, half-brother to hurdles winner, ground query

runners.name: Colugo
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat; tailed off on hurdles debut at Sligo; huge improvement required.
runners.comment: Modest on the Flat, tailed off on hurdles bow at Sligo, huge improvement required

runners.name: Ellens Pride
runners.spotlight: First reserve; point winner on yielding ground for Tim Doyle when last seen in November; well beaten all three starts under rules, in two bumpers and a Tramore maiden hurdle; best watched.
runners.comment: Point winner, struggled in three previous starts under Rules, best watched; reserve

runners.name: Little Big John
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; pulled up and tailed off in maiden hurdles at Fairyhouse and Clonmel; new headgear on; others preferred.
runners.comment: Pulled up and tailed off first two starts, new headgear combo, others preferred; reserve

runners.name: Laligurash
runners.spotlight: Unseated in both starts in points; tailed off on hurdling debut at Cork; unlikely winner.
runners.comment: Unseated in both points, tailed off in maiden hurdle at Cork, unlikely winner

runners.name: Ancient Myth
runners.spotlight: Flat winner for David O'Meara was tailed off when 50/1 on stable/hurdles debut at Killarney; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Flat winner in Britain, tailed off on hurdles/stable debut latest, hard to fancy

runners.name: Blinding Light
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three maiden hurdles last year; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Tailed off at huge odds in trio of maiden hurdles last year, impossible to fancy

races.race_name: Monroe's Live John & Margaret Monroe Memorial Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Astelia
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in five of her seven hurdle starts; may score now after a solid display at Down Royal on her first run since November; suited by good ground.
runners.comment: Runner-up in five of her seven hurdle starts may score after a solid display at Down Royal

runners.name: Kabylia
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; placed on all four maiden hurdle starts; well beaten in two handicap hurdle attempts; still relatively early days, so should not be ruled out.
runners.comment: Consistent in bumpers and maiden hurdles, well beaten in two handicap hurdle attempts

runners.name: Paul's Dream
runners.spotlight: Mostly reliable in the first half of last season; enjoyed a fine spell during which she notched two wins and two seconds; may show the benefit of a recent outing at Killarney.
runners.comment: Mostly reliable in the first half of last season, may show the benefit of a recent outing

runners.name: Miss Manzor
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in three different categories (hurdle/Flat/chase) on her last three starts; needs to show signs of a revival before becoming a betting proposition again.
runners.comment: Has struggled in three different categories (hurdle/Flat/chase) on her last three starts

runners.name: Grainne A Chroi
runners.spotlight: Shown a good standard of form in mares' races, notably when winning the Grade 3 Quevega at Punchestown (2m4f, soft); out of her depth against high-class geldings last time; probably best over further and with an ease.
runners.comment: Good standard in mares' races, probably best over further and on easier ground
runners.owner: Ms Eileen O'Brien & John Anthony Harrington

runners.name: Regards To Rose
runners.spotlight: Fairly consistent before winning at Listowel last September, may need this after a break.
runners.comment: Fairly consistent before winning at Listowel last September, may need this after a break
runners.owner: Exors of the Late M Mortell

runners.name: Eye Of A Tiger
runners.spotlight: Solid form in the first half of last season; out of form since winning at Kilbeggan last August; unseated in a chase last week.
runners.comment: Solid form in the first half of last season, out of form, unseated in a chase last week

runners.name: Beauforts Storm
runners.spotlight: Reverts to hurdling after an unsuccessful spell over fences; likes good ground but it remains to be seen if she can recapture useful 2024 form.
runners.comment: Reverts to hurdling after an unsuccessful spell over fences, likes good ground

runners.name: You Make Me Smile
runners.spotlight: Has been running well on the Flat; fair hurdles record but 13lb out of the handicap here; tough task in the circumstances, though rider's 5lb allowance will help.
runners.comment: Has been running well on the Flat, fair hurdles record but 13lb out of the handicap
runners.owner: T & R Hennessy & A Fahy Partnership

runners.name: Queenofthelodge
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last year; gained third win in 2m4f Clonmel mares' event in October; has been found wanting in conditions races since; may have needed recent Killarney run.
runners.comment: Has been found wanting in conditions races since winning a Clonmel handicap off 10lb lower

races.race_name: Portwest Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bhean Saibhre
runners.spotlight: Won 18.5f maiden hurdle here last August (good); fit from the Flat when beaten 11l in Kilbeggan handicap hurdle on latest; same mark here; a bit more needed.
runners.comment: Course winner at 18.5f, beaten off this mark at Kilbeggan latest, more needed

runners.name: Mon Sheriffe
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 0-12 over hurdles; ran well on testing ground at Navan in December but has failed to complete two of last three starts; cheekpieces tried now; others preferred.
runners.comment: Pulled up in two of last three starts, others make more appeal; cheekpieces on; reserve
runners.owner: T Leopold, P Smith, D P Connolly & C Connolly

runners.name: Cool Summer
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; has failed to build on last year's promising Roscommon maiden hurdle third; best watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Failed to build on last year's Roscommon third, watch in first handicap; reserve

runners.name: Piccolo Player
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground maiden hurdle winner at Limerick (2m) in November; seemed to handle quicker ground when a 7.5l runner up at Kilbeggan latest (16.5f, gd-yld); place shout again off 1lb higher for yard with good record here.
runners.comment: Raised 1lb for Kilbeggan second latest, yard has good record here, place claims

runners.name: Bowgate Street
runners.spotlight: First reserve; belated first win at Sligo on latest (2m2f, good) 14 days ago; raised 7lb, this is a tougher race and not sure the drop back in trip is ideal.
runners.comment: Up 7lb for 2m2f Sligo win, this harder, drop in trip not sure to suit; reserve

runners.name: Desert Haven
runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner is 0-12 over hurdles but has run well in defeat; beaten 0.75l off 2lb lower in this race last May (good); decent effort at Kilbeggan latest when 3.5l behind Piccolo Player; should go well with blinkers back on.
runners.comment: Beaten 0.75l in this last year off 2lb lower, decent run at Kilbeggan latest, go well

runners.name: Kelly's Cosmos
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran well at Galway and Limerick in final two runs of 2025 but hasn't matched those efforts the last twice; beaten 19l when sixth at Cork latest; new hood needs to eke out more.
runners.comment: Promise at backend of 2025, below best last twice, new hood needs to eke out more

runners.name: Can Happen
runners.spotlight: Has run well in defeat at times in maiden hurdles but struggled badly in first two handicaps at 2m7f-3m; big drop in trip here on first start for new yard; watch.
runners.comment: Heavy handicap defeats over further last twice, new yard, down in trip, watch
runners.owner: Michael J Mulligan & Patrick G Walsh

runners.name: Free Flowing
runners.spotlight: Has lost way since a Galway maiden hurdle win in October 2023; no sign of revival in first-time cheekpieces (retained) in Clonmel beginners' chase latest; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Lost way since 2023 Galway maiden hurdle win, no signs of a revival on latest

runners.name: Ned In The Park
runners.spotlight: Off the mark when making all off 5lb lower at Clonmel on penultimate (2m4f, yielding); beaten off revised mark back there latest; ran respectably both previous visits to this venue and drop to 2m might suit; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Made all at Clonmel off 5lb lower on penultimate, drops in trip, has run ok here before

runners.name: Moylussa
runners.spotlight: 1-10 over hurdles but without a win since scoring in a Tipperary maiden (16.5f, soft) in October 2024; not a bad run at Cork on penultimate (2m4f, yielding) but didn't back it up at Punchestown latest; others look more solid.
runners.comment: No win since 2024, not a bad run penultimate, didn't back it up latest, others more solid

runners.name: Kazakh d'Arthel
runners.spotlight: Won four-runner novice chase at Cork (16.5f, good) in October; 0-6 over hurdles but has run well in defeat; beaten 5.25l when fourth off this mark at Navan in September; fell in a handicap chase at Navan latest; not discounted back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Winning chaser, has run well in defeat over hurdles, fell in chase latest, not discounted

runners.name: Atlantic Gamble
runners.spotlight: Flat winner is 0-5 over hurdles and was well beaten in sole previous handicap off 3lb higher at Limerick on penultimate; best form has come on heavy ground; others preferred.
runners.comment: Flat winner, no show in five starts over hurdles, best form on heavy, others preferred

runners.name: Mary's Pride
runners.spotlight: Is 6lb lower than when gaining second win over hurdles at Tipperary in July 2024; soundly beaten off 100 when last seen at Down Royal in December; 1lb lower now and has gone well fresh, so not dismissed in first-time cheekpieces.
