runners.comment: 6lb below last win, lacks a run but has gone well fresh, cheekpieces on, can't dismiss

runners.name: Cornaroya Rocco
runners.spotlight: Improved efforts in maidens at Tramore and Kilbeggan the last twice; Darragh O'Keeffe keeps the faith on handicap debut and opening mark looks fair; not discounted.
runners.comment: Improved maiden efforts last twice, O'Keeffe keeps faith, opening mark looks fair

runners.name: Allo Al Khawaneej
runners.spotlight: Modest on the Flat; 1-6 over hurdles, the win coming in a Kilbeggan maiden in April 2025 (15.5f, gd-yld); heavy ground not ideal the last twice; could improve for this sounder surface.
runners.comment: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner, testing ground not ideal last twice, may do better now

runners.name: Valsorpresa
runners.spotlight: Won a Downpatrick maiden hurdle for Gordon Elliott in April 2024; no joy since but latest Galway fifth (2m, gd-yld) in October his best since joining this yard; dropped 3lb and sports a tongue-tie for return; could run into a place if fit.
runners.comment: Best effort for current yard on latest in Oct', tongue tie on, minor place shout if fit

runners.name: Wandering Rocks
runners.spotlight: Dual purpose; won a C&D maiden hurdle (yielding) in May 2024; raced exclusively on the Flat in 2026; cheekpieces replace blinkers worn for last two starts; worth a market check on return to hurdling.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, fit from the Flat, worth a market check on return to hurdling

runners.name: Expert Analysis
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; very modest form in both codes; can only be watched in first handicap hurdle with cheekpieces applied.
runners.comment: Modest form on Flat and over hurdles, cheekpieces on, watch on handicap debut

races.race_name: FBD Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ladiam
runners.spotlight: 2-28 with both wins coming at Sligo, the most recent off 2lb lower last June (20.5f, good); has run well over C&D off higher marks in the past and likes good ground; go well.
runners.comment: Two-time Sligo winner, has run well over C&D off higher marks, likes good ground

runners.name: Aine's Lad
runners.spotlight: In good form in 2026; runner-up at Punchestown (2m7f, heavy), then won off 9lb lower at Thurles (2m7f, soft) in February; respectable chase run at Fairyhouse latest (3m, heavy); may need softer ground than this.
runners.comment: In good form in 2026, won off 9lb lower at Thurles in Feb', may need softer ground

runners.name: Survivors Sister
runners.spotlight: Sole win came off 9lb lower at Limerick in December (2m5f, heavy); beaten off revised mark thrice since and was beaten 8.75l when third at Sligo latest; could do with some rain.
runners.comment: No impact off revised mark since December win at Limerick, could do with some rain
runners.owner: John J McGrath & T C Quinn

runners.name: It's Time Again
runners.spotlight: 3-25 with all three wins coming at this track, the most recent in April last year off this mark; should be approaching peak fitness after two recent runs and can't be discounted back at her favourite venue.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner, on last winning mark, can't discount back at favourite track

runners.name: My Cullen
runners.spotlight: Maiden was placed in a couple of handicaps last year for Henrietta Knight; poor form in three starts for this yard; new cheekpieces need to spark.
runners.comment: Maiden, poor form in three starts for this yard, new cheekpieces need to spark

runners.name: Tomas O Maille
runners.spotlight: Locally-trained C&D winner off 3lb higher last April; probably needed the run on return from a break at Sligo latest; course form figures over timber on ground with good in description read 55142; big player.
runners.comment: 3lb below last year's C&D win, likes good ground, sharper for latest, big player

runners.name: Nothing Hectic
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D maiden hurdle (good) last August; pulled up and beaten 20l in two handicap starts since then; may have needed latest, however, and could do better returned to this venue.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner last August, may have needed latest, could do better back here

runners.name: Andyourbirdcansing
runners.spotlight: First reserve; Sligo maiden hurdle winner in 2024; not a bad run on return from a break at Clonmel latest and handicapper giving her a chance, but stamina a query over this trip.
runners.comment: Sligo maiden hurdle winner, not a bad run latest, stamina a query at this trip; reserve

runners.name: Jisco Du Brem
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner last June; 0-6 over hurdles but has shown enough to suggest he can win races; beaten 16l when fifth of 15 over 2m3f at Cork latest; shaped nicely over 3m in Wexford novice previously so longer trip here a plus; chance.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, beaten 16l over 2m3f latest, longer trip here a plus, chance

runners.name: Listentomewillya
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 0-5 and yet to display any signs of winning potential; may need a bit more time and experience.
runners.comment: 0-5, no signs yet of winning potential, may need more time and experience; reserve

runners.name: Obey The Order
runners.spotlight: Promise in a couple of maidens for Philip Rothwell; pulled up when last seen in a Thurles novice in December; has joined top yard ahead of handicap debut; check market.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens, pulled up when last seen in Dec', new yard, check market
runners.owner: D L'Estrange & D L'Estrange & N Courtney & Cillian Moran

runners.name: Empire Walk
runners.spotlight: Best run in maidens an 8l second of 16 at Naas in December (2m3f, sft-hvy); handled quicker ground when a 4.75l third in second handicap start at Cork (24.5f, good) 16 days ago; this shorter trip will suit on the evidence of that run; in the mix.
runners.comment: Good effort on second handicap start at Cork, drop in trip here a plus, in the mix

runners.name: Brave Crogha
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner in 2024; 0-7 over hurdles and yet to trouble the judge; off since heavy defeat at Galway in October; 3lb drop not enough to tempt on return.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, 0-7 over hurdles, heavy defeat when last seen in Oct', watch

runners.name: Charlie's Dilemma
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over timber but has placed six times in maiden hurdles; sole handicap start came at Musselburgh in February and was soundly beaten; similar outcome in Tramore maiden hurdle latest; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Placed in six maiden hurdles, well beaten in sole h'cap at Musselburgh, others preferred

runners.name: Lady Bluebird
runners.spotlight: In fine form; won at Tramore (2m5f, gd-yld) in April; another good effort off 7lb higher mark at Cork (2m3f, good); step up in trip a plus; career-best needed off this mark; due to run here 5.35 Monday.
runners.comment: In fine form, won at Tramore, runner-up at Cork, career-best needed off this mark

runners.name: Portarlington
runners.spotlight: 3-20 over hurdles; shaped like the run was needed after six months off at Sligo on latest when 33-1; remains 2lb above last win and might need another run or two to hit top form.
runners.comment: 33-1 and well beaten after break at Sligo, remains 2lb above last win

runners.name: Low Style
runners.spotlight: Represents last year's winning yard; 1-10, the win coming in 2022 at Roscommon (16.5f, good) off 10lb lower; stays this far and shaped ok at Punchestown on latest, staying on over 2m (gd-yld); could go well back up in trip.
runners.comment: No win since 2022, stayed on nicely over 2m latest, yard won this last year

runners.name: Elbow Lane
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; didn't show much in maidens and ordinary effort on handicap debut at Clonmel in April (2m, soft); still early days but best watched up in trip on first start on good ground.
runners.comment: No show in maidens or h'cap debut, new trip/ground here, best watched; reserve

runners.name: Ellaat
runners.spotlight: Dual purpose; course Flat winner hasn't won a handicap hurdle since 2022 and made an inauspicious start for this yard when failing to beat a rival on the Flat at Limerick; will strip fitter now but others preferred.
runners.comment: No handicap hurdle win since 2022, poor start for this yard on Flat latest

races.race_name: Irishinjuredjockeys.com Optional Claiming Race

runners.name: The Liffey
runners.spotlight: Won at two and three for Ballydoyle; won claimers here over 1m and 6f in February; coughing post-race when disappointing odds-on favourite in 7f claimer here in March; been given time to recover and looks a big player.
runners.comment: Won claimers here over 1m and 6f in February; coughing post-race when disappointing latest

runners.name: Battle Borne
runners.spotlight: Won modest 7f maiden here in February but soundly beaten on AW and turf since.
runners.comment: Won modest 7f maiden here in February but soundly beaten on AW and turf since

runners.name: Wolfpack
runners.spotlight: Formerly owned by Qatar Racing and, having been placed on Tapeta at two, won over 1m at Thirsk last summer; showed a bit on hurdling debut, tailed off thrice over flights since; hard to gauge.
runners.comment: Placed on Tapeta at two and won over 1m at Thirsk last year; tailed off hurdling lately

runners.name: Independent Expert
runners.spotlight: Eight wins, two of them here; latest success was over 7f at Naas in March and ran well in big-field contest at the Curragh next time; disappointed when odds-on for Gowran claimer last week, but picked up a few grazes and she's better than that.
runners.comment: Eight wins, two of them here; disappointed when odds-on at Gowran but had an excuse

runners.name: Land Of The Giants
runners.spotlight: Plenty of placed form here over various trips; had been in good form on turf, including breakthrough win over 1m1f at Musselburgh, before poor run last time when only 5-1 for Leopardstown handicap.
runners.comment: Stall advantaged over trip, expected to go really well
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith & Bart O'Sullivan

runners.name: Cosmic Force
runners.spotlight: Placed in 7f handicap here last month; ratings don't matter much in claimers, but couldn't entertain 41-rated 4yo all the same.
runners.comment: Placed in 7f handicap here last month, but couldn't entertain 41-rated 4yo all the same

runners.name: Yosemite Gold
runners.spotlight: 107-rated hurdler won a maiden in that sphere last summer; ordinary enough Flat form and doubt dropping back from last two runs over 1m4f will be the answer.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler has ordinary enough Flat form and doubt dropping to 1m will be the answer

runners.name: Imperial Fighter
runners.spotlight: Well exposed but won on Tapeta at Wolverhampton last year; second to a subsequent improver over C&D in January; poor form since.
runners.comment: Won on Tapeta at Wolverhampton last year,  second over C&D in January; poor form since

runners.name: North Ocean
runners.spotlight: Well bred and cost 550,000gns as a yearling, so they didn't expect to end up here; ran fine over C&D sole start at two; wasn't seen until 4-1 at Ballinrobe earlier this month, beaten a long way over 9.5f; maybe he didn't stay, but that might be too generous an assessment.
runners.comment: Ran fine over C&D sole start at two; beaten a long way over 9.5f at Ballinrobe

runners.name: Albion Power
runners.spotlight: C&D handicap winner in November and fair fourth here in March; mid-field in Curragh handicap on Sunday.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November and fair fourth here in March; mid-field at the Curragh on Sunday

runners.name: Ledger
runners.spotlight: Won four times in Britain for Lucinda Russell, but form has fallen off a cliff since rejoining this yard.
runners.comment: Won four times in Britain, but form has fallen off a cliff since rejoining this yard

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Jamestown
runners.spotlight: Beaten by the same horse in his last two races, latterly in a valuable handicap at Naas (1m, good); that form just about sets the standard back in a maiden and dropping to 7f won't hurt; had a wasted journey to Haydock at the weekend where he was due to line up in a 100k handicap.
runners.comment: Beaten by the same horse in his last two races, latterly in a valuable handicap at Naas

runners.name: Rayzera
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but heading in the right direction after chasing home a very talented Ballydoyle odds-on favourite at Limerick (7f); unlikely she faces anything of that calibre here and has solid claims.
runners.comment: 0-4 but best run yet last time when second to a Ballydoyle odds-on favourite at Limerick
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Fate's Gambit
runners.spotlight: Hasn't progressed from his second here on debut and again looks vulnerable in a maiden.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed from his second here on debut and again looks vulnerable in a maiden
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell

runners.name: Ribble Emperor
runners.spotlight: Has placed form here but he's now 0-8 and exposed; unlikely to be winning this.
runners.comment: Has placed form here but he's now 0-8 and exposed; unlikely to be winning this

runners.name: Nautic Star
runners.spotlight: Very likeable debut when a keeping-on fifth behind none other than Diamond Necklace in a 7f good-ground maiden at the Curragh last August and perhaps the soft ground found her out next time; brings potential into her second season; market useful.
runners.comment: Likeable debut effort behind Diamond Necklace; soft ground an excuse next time
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Henry Hudson
runners.spotlight: Went off 50-1 when beaten 10l over 1m at Killarney and looks one for down the line.
runners.comment: Went off 50-1 when beaten 10l over 1m at Killarney and looks one for down the line

runners.name: Make The Tide
runners.spotlight: From off the pace she finished in mid-field at Leopardstown (1m; 25-1), showing some promise; still looks one for another day.
runners.comment: From off the pace she finished in midfield at Leopardstown (1m), showing some promise

runners.name: Gilts
runners.spotlight: Insufficient promise in her first two races to warrant a second glance in this maiden.
runners.comment: Insufficient promise in her first two races to warrant a second glance in this maiden

runners.name: Kirkland Sioux
runners.spotlight: Lost all chance at the start and was behind throughout at Leopardstown (1m, good); didn't appear unfancied in the betting at 13-2 and, if all the wiser this time, could take a marked step forward.
runners.comment: Lost all chance at the start and behind throughout at Leopardstown; did go off at 13-2

runners.name: Miss Mercier
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in all her races, including a claimer; zero appeal.
runners.comment: Tailed off in all her races, including a claimer; zero appeal

runners.name: Themis
runners.spotlight: Her Irish Oaks entry is unrealistic on what she's shown in her first two races; handicaps will be more like it.
runners.comment: Her Irish Oaks entry is unrealistic on what she's shown in her first two races

runners.name: Unidos
runners.spotlight: Some promise when beaten under 5l on Naas debut (6f) and the ground was heavy when tailed off at the Curragh; one to watch on his comeback run having been gelded.
runners.comment: Minor promise as a juvenile; one to watch on his comeback run having been gelded

races.race_name: Moffett - The Competitive Edge Maiden

runners.name: Aughlish
runners.spotlight: Bred for AW and three solid runs here, including second on only start over 5f; soft ground may have been her undoing at Sligo and cheekpieces tried there now discarded; draw no help but should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Bred for AW and three solid runs here, including second on only start over 5f; thereabouts

runners.name: Cool Azul
runners.spotlight: Has been on the speed in two 6f runs on soft; second of 24 at the Curragh (RPR of 83) and no match for promising newcomer when second at Cork; speedy pedigree and shorter trip should be fine; big chance if he handles the surface.
runners.comment: Second of 24 at the Curragh and no match for promising newcomer when second at Cork

runners.name: Sparky Sparky
runners.spotlight: Has only run on soft turf; first two runs were fine, including comeback third over 5f at Cork; 10-11 fav back there over 6f, was on the wrong side when bombed out completely in race where Cool Azul was second; that was too bad to be true; tongue-tie on now.
runners.comment: Has only run on soft turf; first two runs were fine; 10-11 fav  when bombed out at Cork

runners.name: Reposado
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer was second over this trip at two; second over 6f three times this year, once here and in big-field Curragh handicaps last twice.
runners.comment: Second over 6f three times, once here and in big-field Curragh handicaps last twice
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Focaccia
runners.spotlight: Only 4-1 when promising third in 6f Curragh maiden in March; out the back in stakes race last time; flashed tail both starts and certainly doesn't like the persuader; Heffernan will be donning kid gloves for this one.
runners.comment: Third in 6f Curragh maiden in March; has flashed tail both starts

runners.name: Beach Barbie
runners.spotlight: Dam was decent and won over 6f here but has been a poor broodmare thus far; has been behind Aughlish both starts here, just over two lengths off her over this trip last time.
runners.comment: Has been behind Aughlish both starts, just over two lengths off her over C&D last time
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & Edward Lynam

runners.name: Banshof
runners.spotlight: Hello Youmzain gelding is closely related to 7f winner The Attorney (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 103), half-brother to 5f-7f winner Taj Alriyadh (inc 2yo/HK Group 3; 117); dam unraced half-sister to winners Space Blues (6.5f/7f Group 1) and Shuruq (UAE 1m Group 2); 260,000gns yearling didn't make it to the track for the Crisfords; picked up for small money in October.
runners.comment: Well-bred 260,000gns yearling was unraced for the Crisfords; picked up for small money

runners.name: Auguste Gusteau
runners.spotlight: Raced keenly when beaten 6l over 6f here in February; 50-1 when wiped out at the start at Navan lately.
runners.comment: Beaten 6l over 6f here in February;  50-1 when wiped out at the start at Navan lately
runners.owner: One Eight Seven Four Syndicate/Paul J Cahill

runners.name: Allirelandmaterial
runners.spotlight: Phoenix Of Spain filly; E17,000 yearling; half-sister to Italian winners Wait Forever (7.5f-1m1f inc 2yo/Italian 2,000 Guineas) and Kensington Square (1m-1m2f); dam 7f AW winner (82); 8-1 at Navan, took no part after being wiped out at the start, so this is effectively her debut.
runners.comment: 8-1 at Navan, took no part after being wiped out at the start, this effectively her debut

runners.name: Lamar Canyon
runners.spotlight: Well beaten first two starts but showed some ability in 6f winners' race here on latest outing in November; first runner for trainer and first go over 5f.
runners.comment: Showed some ability in 6f winners' race here in November; first runner for trainer

races.race_name: Put The Fun In Fundraising At Dundalk Handicap

runners.name: Real Encounter
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced filly from a top yard; responded well for pressure to win her maiden over C&D but lacked the gears to land a blow when upped to 6f at Naas (handicap debut); could be given another chance back here.
runners.comment: Only sixth of 14 on handicap debut at Naas but she won her maiden over C&D

runners.name: Buddy Batt
runners.spotlight: Only fifth on soft ground last time but he had previously made it 3-7 here when cosily accounting for Lethimfly in March; only 3lb higher now but his wide draw is worrying.
runners.comment: Returning here has to be a good thing but his wide draw is worrying

runners.name: Collective Power
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner on a good mark and was close up behind Clonmacash on his last visit here, since when he's finished ninth at Naas; like others in here, this place is more his scene.
runners.comment: Always respected here; close up behind Clonmacash on his last visit in April
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Cuban Grey
runners.spotlight: Has a superior strike-rate on turf and was behind three of these on his last run here in March.
runners.comment: Superior strike-rate on turf and was behind three of these on his last run here in March

runners.name: All The Girls
runners.spotlight: Looked useful when winning her maiden here over 7f but hasn't progressed as expected; didn't run at all badly in the new cheekpieces when beaten 3l at Naas (6f) but this will be her first time at 5f.
runners.comment: Looked useful when winning her maiden here over 7f but hasn't progressed as expected
runners.owner: Elaine Shevlin & Cormac Farrell & Ms Denise McCourt

runners.name: Sovereign Cry
runners.spotlight: Progressive profile here last season, beating an 82-rated filly the final time; the glaring concern is his recent heavy defeat at Naas.
runners.comment: Won here last season but the glaring concern is his recent heavy defeat at Naas
runners.owner: John B O'Hagan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Arctic Steps
runners.spotlight: Has a solid five-race record at this track and returns after winning for a second time on turf at Naas (5f), beating 15 rivals in the process under Patrick McGettigan; has a 6lb rise to deal with, but she's not exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Has a solid five-race record at this track and won on turf last time; now 6lb higher

runners.name: Clonmacash
runners.spotlight: Behind Buddy Batt and Lethimfly here in March but later posted a career-best RPR when winning over 6f back here in April and can be forgiven his subsequent defeat at Chester; hard to leave out of calculations.
runners.comment: Posted a career-best RPR when winning over 6f here in April; Chester run forgiven

runners.name: Jered Maddox
runners.spotlight: Seeking an 11th course success, having gained his tenth by a narrow margin over 6f here in April (fine at 5f); only 2lb higher and wouldn't read too much into a no-show at the Curragh last time.
runners.comment: Always of interest around here and only 2lb higher than for his win in April

runners.name: Sporting Hero
runners.spotlight: Won for a first time on turf at Navan (5f) but then finished last at Naas; loves it around here but stall 11 looks troublesome.
runners.comment: Recent turf winner who loves it around here but stall 11 looks troublesome

runners.name: Lethimfly
runners.spotlight: 5f maiden winner at two and confined to only two runs last season; however, plenty to like about his close second behind Buddy Batt here in March and he's better drawn this time should they again wish to front run.
runners.comment: Plenty to like about his close second behind Buddy Batt here in March

runners.name: Billboa
runners.spotlight: All three wins came here in successive races, the latest in March 2024; hasn't looked the same horse since returning from an absence.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner but hasn't looked the same horse since returning from an absence

runners.name: Harry's Hill
runners.spotlight: Better known for his turf exploits and he's 1-8 on the AW; others preferred after finishing down the field at Navan after a break.
runners.comment: Others preferred after finishing down the field at Navan after a break

races.race_name: Sunday 12th July Ladies Day At Dundalk Stadium Maiden

runners.name: Cite Florale
runners.spotlight: Okay debut here over 1m in November; ran much better than her 80-1 price predicted when beaten 3l over extended 1m2f at Naas last month; winner went in again next time and now rated 92; related to pair of useful AW winners over this trip.
runners.comment: Beaten 3l over extended 1m2f at Naas;  related to useful AW winners over this trip.

runners.name: Minella Rockett
runners.spotlight: Beaten a length, then a head, in pair of 1m maidens here in November; on recent return, was too keen down the hill in the early stages at Navan on handicap debut over 1m2f; stamina-laden Aga Khan pedigree on dam's side but needs to settle.
runners.comment: Went close in two 1m maidens in November; too keen over 1m2f on recent return to action

runners.name: Believed
runners.spotlight: Frankel half-sister to four winners, notably 2022 Melbourne Cup hero Gold Trip; looked raw when fourth here over 10.5f; hood went on at Leopardstown, chased home all-the-way winner over 1m2f, plugging away; entered in Group 1 Pretty Polly; good chance this longer trip will suit.
runners.comment: Chased home all-the-way winner over 1m2f at Leopardstown, plugging away; trip should suit

runners.name: Diligo
runners.spotlight: Green on debut at Gowran, slow to break and wandering before running on strongly into fourth late on (9.5f); well-held third in cheekpieces over 1m5f at Ballinrobe, where track may not have been ideal; tongue-tie now added; half-brother won on AW.
runners.comment: Well-held third in cheekpieces over 1m5f at Ballinrobe; that track may not have suited
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Yogini
runners.spotlight: Homebred by Waldgeist; brother to 1m4f winner Atlantic Sunset (RPR 81), closely related to Group 1 winners Forever Together (1m4f Oaks; 117) and Together Forever (Fillies' Mile; 110), half-brother to six winners including Lord Shanakill (1m Group 1; 119); dam unraced; tongue-tie on for debut.
runners.comment: Homebred by Waldgeist is related to several Group1 winners; no fancy entries; tongue-tie

runners.name: Werifesteria
runners.spotlight: By Preakness winner War Of Will who is a half-brother to yard's Group 1 winner Pathfork; half-sister to winners Solar Halo (6f; RPR 89), In The Present (7f AW 2yo; 88), Landbiscuit (US 1m-1m1f turf/dirt/AW) and Yulong Storm (Australian 5.5f-6.5f); dam 1m AW 2yo winner (81), closely related to Derby winner Kris Kin; taken out of recent intended debut due to testing ground; headgear on.
runners.comment: By Preakness winner War Of Will and is half-sister to four winners; headgear on

runners.name: Police Gazette
runners.spotlight: 28-1 second string on debut, never a factor over 1m here last month and headgear left off now; homebred son of War Front with plenty of stamina on dam's side.
runners.comment: 28-1 second string on debut, never a factor over 1m here last month

runners.name: Teoest
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 1m2f winner Ambitious Approach (RPR 79); dam unplaced 7f 2yo (49), is granddaughter of 1m Group 3 winning half-sister to Derby winner New Approach; bought back for 4,000 euros as yearling.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 1m2f winner Ambitious Approach (RPR 79); family of New Approach

runners.name: Avoca Woods
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in two turf maidens; up in trip for AW debut.
runners.comment: Big prices and well beaten in two turf maidens; up in trip for AW debut

runners.name: Tanzanite Diamond
runners.spotlight: Nicely-bred gelding was beaten 14l over 1m2f at Navan; will need more experience.
runners.comment: Nicely-bred gelding was beaten 14l over 1m2f at Navan; will need more experience

runners.name: Nosmokewithoutfire
runners.spotlight: Out the back in two turf maidens; handicap mark after this.
runners.comment: Out the back in two turf maidens; handicap mark after this
runners.owner: Ms Denise McCourt & Stephen Sweeney & Maire Bailey

races.race_name: Family Fun Day Saturday 15th August Handicap

runners.name: Skib Gold
runners.spotlight: Won at a price over Gowran's extended 1m1f last summer and was second there next time off this mark; however, has looked cold as ice this season.
runners.comment: Has the form to be competitive but he's looked as cold as ice this season

runners.name: Grey Fable
runners.spotlight: Both wins came at Chester in 2024; it's been a washout so far this season and remains best watched.
runners.comment: It's been a washout so far this season and remains best watched

runners.name: Copper Craft
runners.spotlight: 0-7; sent off favourite over 1m2f here a month ago and wasn't beaten far into third behind Jawhary; that was after a break, so might be sharper this time.
runners.comment: Third to Jawhary last month and that was after a break, so may be sharper now

runners.name: Kilgharrahs Love
runners.spotlight: Lost way last year and no real signs of a revival this campaign, including in this headgear combination last time.
runners.comment: Lost way last year and no real signs of a revival this campaign

runners.name: Eastern Wind
runners.spotlight: Behind Jawhary and Copper Craft here in April, taking AW record to 0-9 (no places); more effective on turf, as recent Leopardstown second reminded us.
runners.comment: Recent second on turf but that's more his surface; 0-9 on AW and yet to place

runners.name: Sarmiento Power
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in September off 10lb lower but doesn't appear to be in anything like the same form currently; tries a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: C&D winner in September but doesn't appear to be in anything like the same form currently

runners.name: Highway Sixty One
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden for Andrew McNamara; did finish third in a big field over this far at Fairyhouse; best watched on yard debut after a lengthy break.
runners.comment: Some form offers hope but best watched on yard debut after a lengthy break

runners.name: Numidia
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on turf in his last two races but he's much better on the AW and wasn't beaten far here in March; considered.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on turf in his last two races but he's much better on the AW

runners.name: Angel Harry
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced maiden but hasn't got anywhere near the principals in any of his races, including two handicaps; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden but hasn't got anywhere near the principals in any of his races

runners.name: Chopsticks
runners.spotlight: Hasn't taken to hurdles since last seen on the Flat and he's only 1-25 overall, including 13 defeats here; however, has placed form at Dundalk, including off this mark in September; couldn't put a line through him.
runners.comment: Has been hurdling; placed form at Dundalk, including off this mark in September

runners.name: Jawhary
runners.spotlight: Down the field on turf last time but had been running well here and should still be on a competitive mark; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Down the field on turf last time but had been running well here; on the shortlist

runners.name: Smooth
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has shown nothing of note in handicaps and stamina isn't guaranteed.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden who has shown nothing of note in handicaps and stamina isn't guaranteed

runners.name: Tara Power
runners.spotlight: Reserve; both wins have been here but 2-52 overall and current wellbeing is an issue.
runners.comment: Reserve; both wins have been here but 2-52 overall and current wellbeing is an issue

runners.name: Church Mountain
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner on AW/turf but mostly disappointing since his C&D win in January, nine runs back; bit risky.
runners.comment: Mostly disappointing since his C&D win in January, nine runs back; bit risky

runners.name: Folk Warrior
runners.spotlight: No worthwhile form in maidens on the Flat and over hurdles; much to prove on handicap debut.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form in maidens on the Flat and over hurdles; handicap debut

races.race_name: View Restaurant At Dundalk Stadium Maiden

runners.name: Mano Chicago
runners.spotlight: Twice finished third at two and missed all of last year; first two runs this term were good, including fifth in decent handicap at Cork; in first-time blinkers, was no match for a patiently-ridden winner after trying to make all at Ballinrobe; first go on Polytrack and related to AW winners.
runners.comment: No match for a patiently-ridden winner after leading at Ballinrobe; related to AW winners
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Therewillbeglory
runners.spotlight: Runner-up first four starts last year (7f/1m) and maybe heavy didn't suit on her final start over 9.5f; half-sister won on AW and winning stayers in family.
runners.comment: Runner-up first four starts last year (7f/1m) and maybe heavy didn't suit on final start

runners.name: Take Me
runners.spotlight: Coughed after poor return, so forget that; best run last year was strong-finishing fifth at Navan (1m2f, good); tongue-tie on for AW debut and dam won 5f maiden here.
runners.comment: Coughed after poor return, so forget that; best run last year was fifth over 1m2f at Navan

runners.name: Kilcrea Rock
runners.spotlight: Ran well on seasonal debut when second in 1m2f Leopardstown handicap on good ground; disappointing on softer terrain since; AW debut.
runners.comment: Second in 1m2f Leopardstown handicap on good disappointing on softer since; AW debut

runners.name: Western Bandit
runners.spotlight: By a son of Scat Daddy; caught the eye when staying-on fifth over 1m at Limerick (notably round action), but maybe blew his handicap mark too; not so good in tongue-tie since.
runners.comment: Caught the eye when fifth over 1m at Limerick; not so good in tongue-tie since

runners.name: Sron Na Caise
runners.spotlight: Two okay runs on turf; not far behind Mano Chicago over 9.5f on debut, likewise not far off Western Bandit over 1m at Limerick; sire and dam won on AW.
runners.comment: Two okay runs on turf and steps up in trip;  sire and dam won on AW

runners.name: Yangtze River
runners.spotlight: Cheap yearling is half-brother to winners Master Ming (1m 2yo; RPR 84), Heavenly Rainbow (6f 2yo; 80) and Mondorosa (Italian 1m-1m3f inc 2yo); dam 11.4f winner (65), closely related to 1m2f Listed winner Red Fort, out of 1m4f/1m5f winner.
runners.comment: Cheap yearling is half-brother to three winners and is out of 11.4f winner

runners.name: Enchanted Lady
runners.spotlight: Better on second run but still beaten nearly 10l when not seeing out 1m4f at Roscommon.
runners.comment: Better on second run but still beaten nearly 10l when not seeing out 1m4f at Roscommon

runners.name: Jerpoint Abbey
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in a bumper and on Flat debut at Leopardstown; hood and tongue-tie removed and she's likely more of a long-term project.
runners.comment: Ran okay on Flat debut at Leopardstown; hood and tongue-tie removed and best watched

runners.name: Pedro The Poet
runners.spotlight: Has run okay in pair of maidens here, over farther and shorter, but has probably got himself badly handicapped as a result; up against it here.
runners.comment: Has run okay in pair of maidens here, over farther and shorter, but up against it here

runners.name: Chico Chico
runners.spotlight: Nothing so far at massive prices; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Nothing so far at massive prices; tongue-tie added.

runners.name: Steel Cut
runners.spotlight: 22s, showed nothing in Curragh maiden a year ago; half-brother won on AW.
runners.comment: 22s, showed nothing in Curragh maiden a year ago; half-brother won on AW.

races.race_name: Kalm Club @ Hurlands Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kamille Au Kanada
runners.spotlight: First past the post in both Irish points; encouraging 14l fourth in a Listed bumper at Sandown (soft) before failing favourite backers over 2m2f here on good; may prefer softer ground but should get away with it in this company, and the longer trip will suit on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on her bumper exploits and this longer trip should suit

runners.name: Nelson Gate
runners.spotlight: 0-8 on the Flat and 0-5 as a hurdler; he's modest in this discipline but was third in a handicap last time and at least he has jumping experience.
runners.comment: He's modest in this discipline but was third in a handicap last time

runners.name: Arfursgal
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied for all her bumpers; failed to kick on in two good-ground ones after her debut second on soft at Newbury; has been off for 12 months and, with the stable also running Kamille Au Kanada, the market should tell a tale.
runners.comment: Second in one of her bumpers; market informative after a year out and against a stablemate

races.race_name: Bruce Hardwick T'bred Pub In Polegate Cond'nal Jockeys' Training Series Handicap Hurdle (RE Finale)

runners.name: Goodwin
runners.spotlight: He was a hard ride over C&D last month (good) but on top when it mattered and his record here now reads 2111; raised 4lb but a greater concern is whether he'll be up for it.
runners.comment: Now 2111 at this venue but he was a hard ride last month; chance if in the mood

runners.name: Motazzen
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner and career-best form to win by 10l at Fontwell two runs ago (2m5f, good to soft); went up 8lb for that and was back down to earth on his return visit when beaten 20l; hard to know entirely what to expect from him.
runners.comment: Last two runs have seen a career best and a heavy defeat; hard to know what to expect

runners.name: Everyonesgame
runners.spotlight: His form during the opening half of last season included a Kempton win (on good) and a pretty good effort at Cheltenham, both over 3m; this year's two runs were nothing like so good but the ability is there.
runners.comment: Below par in his both his races this year but the ability is there to win this

runners.name: Diplomatic Ash
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner but below his best in two runs over fences during March; returns to hurdling off a realistic mark and has placed form in this discipline.
runners.comment: Chase winner; returns to hurdling off a realistic mark and has placed form in this game

races.race_name: Kalm Club @ Grange Festival Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Seeyouinmydreams
runners.spotlight: 5-11 over hurdles for Paul Nicholls and arrives on the back of a wide-margin success in a seller at Hexham (2m, good) 24 days ago; bought for £26,000 and commands major respect on chase debut for his new stable.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of a seller last time and commands major respect on chase/stable bow.

runners.name: Frere d'Armes
runners.spotlight: Has been well out of form since returning in September from a break and has offered little switched to hunter chases this year; now back in handicap company for his leading stable and down to a potentially workable mark, but recent efforts temper enthusiasm.
runners.comment: Back in handicap company for his leading stable but recent efforts temper enthusiasm

runners.name: Lakefield Flyer
runners.spotlight: Fairly treated on the pick of his better efforts and they were not that far away; shaped with renewed purpose when a respectable fifth back over hurdles at Sligo a fortnight ago and a repeat of that performance back over fences would put him firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Fairly treated on peak form and a better effort last time puts him firmly in the mix.

runners.name: Captain Cool
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; 1-9 over hurdles but progressive overall and got off the mark over fences with a narrow success at Stratford (2m1f, good to firm) nine days ago; leading claims, although a 7lb penalty does ask a stiffer question.
runners.comment: Progressive over hurdles and straight off the mark over fences last time; better to come.

races.race_name: Kalm Club @ Plumpton Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bahtiyar
runners.spotlight: 0-35, often placed; recent respectable efforts suggest he's on a tough mark but he's versatile regarding trip and Murray Dodd can claim his full allowance on this occasion, so he should still be competitive.
runners.comment: On a tough mark but he's been consistent without winning and can play a part.

runners.name: I Shut That d'Or
runners.spotlight: Posted three wins over fences in the winter of 2024-25, all at around this trip, and staged a revival when third at Fontwell (2m4f, good; Begin The Luck second) three weeks ago; back up in class but on a winnable mark and he's one to consider with the stable among the winners.
runners.comment: Back up in class but on a winnable mark for a stable among the winners.

runners.name: Cher Monsieur
runners.spotlight: 1-9 over hurdles; shaped with clear promise on chase debut when second at Uttoxeter (3m, good) 24 days ago; drop in trip shouldn't be an inconvenience and a bold bid to go one better can be expected.
runners.comment: Shaped well on chase debut and a bold bid to go one better can be expected.

runners.name: Begin The Luck
runners.spotlight: Largely reliable veteran and a three-time winner since December, latterly over C&D in April; touched off at Fontwell 20 days ago, with I Shut That d'Or about 5l back in third; nudged up 1lb but likely to make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Nudged up 1lb but he's a reliable veteran who looks sure to make his presence felt again.

races.race_name: Kalm Club @ Welborne Cafe Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Earth King
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over hurdles who had looked to be in the grip of the handicapper since winning at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) in July; however, having come back down in the weights, he stayed on well to regain the winning thread at Hereford (3m1f, good) this month; going back up 4lb is a slight concern, but this looks an inviting opportunity if he repeats that effort.
runners.comment: Gained a fifth hurdle win this month and is a leading contender here despite the 4lb rise.

runners.name: Striking A Pose
runners.spotlight: Recorded his sixth hurdle win when making all off a reduced mark at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) in April, and his follow-up bid off this mark was respectable back there 20 days ago; capable off this mark but not without risk.
runners.comment: Has returned to form of late and he's capable off this mark, but not without risk.
runners.owner: S Clare G Kennington & C Knowles

runners.name: Escapologist
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner for James Owen, most recently at Market Rasen (2m7f, good to soft) in March; backed that up with a solid effort next time but was beaten 22l when favourite back there 18 days ago, which leaves him with a bit to prove; makes stable debut with the booking of Sean Bowen catching the eye.
runners.comment: Form has dipped since his latest win in March, but hard to dismiss on stable debut.

runners.name: Sea Invasion
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come here, three over fences at around 3m and one over hurdles at 2m4f; latest success over fences came here last month in this headgear combination, but he's just as effective over hurdles and a key player reverting to this sphere off a small rise.
runners.comment: Chase winner latest, but equally effective over hurdles and a key player off a small rise.

runners.name: Jubilant
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with emphatic win from the front at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft), his third chase success, but returned from a break with two disappointing runs (chase/hurdle) towards end of last season and has something to prove.
runners.comment: C&D winner in December but out of form since and now has a bit to prove.

races.race_name: Kalm Club - Everyone Welcome Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Goodwin Face
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but he's reached the frame on all four visits here, going close to shedding his maiden tag on two occasions during 2024 and spring 2025; made an encouraging return from an 11-month absence when second over 2m2f here last month and, nudged up 1lb, remains a strong contender dropped in trip.
runners.comment: 0-7 but he made an encouraging return when second here last month and this may be his day.

runners.name: Yasmina
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground C&D winner in February and, switched to handicaps, was only just denied back here in April (good); raised 4lb since that latest effort but remains open to progress and a leading contender, with a promising 5lb claimer booked.
runners.comment: Up 4lb but remains open to progress and a leading contender, with a 5lb claimer booked.

runners.name: Alibey
runners.spotlight: Has taken a marked step forward since switched to handicaps, going close when second over C&D and backing that up with an even better effort when filling the same position at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm) seven days ago; another to consider.
runners.comment: Runner-up on both starts since handicapping and she's likely to be in the mix again.

runners.name: Reteti
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden; 0-6 in this discipline but he's improved for the switch to handicaps and produced a solid effort off this mark when runner-up at Stratford (extended 2m) and coping well with the good to firm ground; can play a major role again.
runners.comment: Solid effort when second off this mark latest and he can play a major role again.

runners.name: Callero
runners.spotlight: Beaten 44l on each of his two completed starts over hurdles and recent Flat form offers little encouragement; switches to handicap company (out of the weights proper) with plenty to prove and it's easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Makes handicap debut but offered little in three runs over hurdles and three on the Flat.

races.race_name: Ladies Day At Season Opener Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Getmyfriend
runners.spotlight: Built on the promise of her March debut (third over C&D) when comfortably opening her account at Southwell (2m, good) a fortnight ago; shoulders a penalty here but may have been found another good opportunity and commands major respect.
runners.comment: Shoulders a penalty here but may have been found another good opportunity; major respect.

runners.name: River Ore
runners.spotlight: Flat bred, half-brother to a Hungarian 6f winner; attracted little support at 22-1 for his Killarney debut 15 days ago (2m1f, good to yielding) but shaped with definite promise; gelded since and likely to come on considerably for that initial race experience.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise on debut and likely to have come on for that initial experience.

runners.name: Parish Girl
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; dam a 1m winner (including AW; RPR 66) and half-sister to winners Wicker (1m), Rochford (7f-1m) and Lady Pimm's (French 1m3f); stable has drawn a blank in bumpers in recent years; jockey arrangements suggest she may be their leading hope, though this test asks a stamina question on debut.
runners.comment: Related to winners at up to 1m3f but stable has drawn a blank in bumpers in recent years.
runners.owner: H J Jarvis & Mrs P Jarvis

runners.name: Zandahar
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to 1m-1m4f winner Salman Bu Thalia; one of two for the stable and likely best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Flat-bred filly; likely best watched on debut for a stable not known for bumper winners.
runners.owner: H J Jarvis & Mrs P Jarvis

runners.name: Scotto
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; brother to hurdle/chase winner Brayhill and half-brother to useful hurdler Chooseyourweapon and a point winner; dam was pulled up in points but is out of a useful bumper and jumps winner; fitted with a tongue-tie on debut and represents a respected yard, with any market support worth noting.
runners.comment: Useful jumps pedigree; from a respected yard on debut and any market support worth noting.

runners.name: Padel Whacker
runners.spotlight: Finished sixth of eight, beaten 24l, at Huntingdon on last month's debut, but was sent off favourite and comes from a family the yard knows well and has enjoyed success with; no surprise to see a much improved showing.
runners.comment: Well held when favourite on last month's debut but no surprise to see better effort here.
runners.owner: J Hinds, A Head & G L Moore

races.race_name: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Across Earth
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler with two wins in that sphere at the end of last year; has winning form over this trip (good to firm) on the Flat and well worth a second look back in this discipline for the first time since October 2024.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner late last year; has winning form over this trip on the Flat

runners.name: Macari
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year including one over hurdles and two over this trip on the Flat; just 2lb above his last winning mark, but needs to put a tame effort at Nottingham (1m6f, good) last month behind him.
runners.comment: Four wins last year, but needs to put a tame effort at Nottingham last month behind him
runners.owner: R Vaughan, R Bisiker, M Jones And S West

runners.name: Russian Rumour
runners.spotlight: Course winner (2m) who bids a for hat-trick after wins at Ffos Las (2m, heavy) last September (by 12l) and on her Nottingham return under Donagh Murphy (1m6f, good) 17 days ago; only 2lb higher and much respected.
runners.comment: Course winner who bids a for hat-trick from a 2lb higher mark; much respected

runners.name: Synergism
runners.spotlight: Won four times last year including over this trip at Sandown (good) in the summer; still 9lb above his last winning mark and off for seven months. but he is proven fresh and this is a drop in class; high on list.
runners.comment: Won four times last year; off seven months, but proven fresh and this is a drop in class

runners.name: Youthful King
runners.spotlight: 4lb lower than when winning over this trip at Sandown (good to firm) last summer, but heavy defeats in his last four outings leave him with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 4lb below last winning mark, but has suffered heavy defeats in his last four starts

races.race_name: Weatherbys Racing Bank Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sunshine Star
runners.spotlight: Narrowly beaten on her Kempton debut (1m, AW) in October, but despite being sent off odds-on she could only manage sixth of ten on her reappearance at Sandown (1m2f, good) last month (winner finished second in the Musidora); worth another chance based on her debut performance.
runners.comment: Just beaten on Kempton debut last autumn but disappointing on return; worth another chance

runners.name: Green Area
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; half-sister to seven winners including Subjectivist (7f-2m4f including AW 2yo/Group 1 Gold Cup; RPR 122), Sir Ron Priestley (1m4f/1m6f including Group 2; 120) and Alba Rose (Group-placed 7f 2yo; 96); dam Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner (104); eye-catching pedigee and market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the Ascot Gold Cup winner Subjectivist; market support should be heeded

runners.name: Bintknight
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns foal; 12-1 when fifth of seven on this month's Ascot debut (1m2f, good); open to improvement, but it will be needed and she may appreciate a bit further.
runners.comment: Fifth of seven on this month's Ascot debut; can improve but may appreciate a bit further

runners.name: Asset
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to six winners including three at Group level; sent off 12-1 when third of six on her Kempton debut (1m, AW) in October, finishing around 3l behind Sunshine Star; out of a middle-distance winner so should cope with the longer trip and the type to do better as a 3yo; respected.
runners.comment: Third of six on her Kempton debut in October; should cope with the longer trip; respected
runners.owner: Gestuet Schlenderhan Gmbh U Co Kg

runners.name: Scarlet Letter
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when beaten a fair way into sixth of seven on her Nottingham debut (1m, good to soft) in October; needs to leave that run well behind.
runners.comment: Beaten a fair way into sixth of seven on her Nottingham debut in October

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Cougar Force
runners.spotlight: Well held in five of her six starts, but only beaten two necks into third of ten in her sole handicap at Bath (5.7f, good) on her penultimate outing/reappearance; each-way claims if recapturing that form.
runners.comment: Well held in five of her six starts, but close third in her sole handicap; each-way claims

runners.name: No Claims Bonus
runners.spotlight: No problem with the longer trip when third of ten at Lingfield (6f, AW) four weeks ago, but still needs to show she is capable of comparative form on turf.
runners.comment: Third of ten on Lingfield AW last time, but yet to show comparative form on turf

runners.name: Saeculamation
runners.spotlight: 1-15, the win coming from 1lb higher at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in December; has run well in both starts since returned to turf last month (not far behind a couple of these on the first occasion), but his draw may prove tricky.
runners.comment: 1lb below sole winning mark; has twice run well back on turf, but has a tricky draw

runners.name: Lesley Buckley
runners.spotlight: Beaten upwards of 12l in five starts over 6f-1m on the AW (visored last time); needs the switch to turf to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Beaten upwards of 12l in five starts on AW; needs switch to turf to make a big difference

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 12th attempt when beating 14 rivals at Nottingham (6f, good) last month (two of these behind), but didn't seem suited by the drop to 5f when fourth of nine there (good) 17 days ago; well drawn for a prominent racer and a major player back up in trip.
runners.comment: Won at Nottingham last month; well drawn for a prominent racer and a major player

runners.name: Creciente
runners.spotlight: Promising as a 2yo last summer, but not built on it and has a bit to find with a couple of these on her two efforts since returning last month; cheekpieces are given a go, but Tickettothestars looks the stable's main hope.
runners.comment: Has a bit to find with a couple of these on her recent form; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Ken Brulee
runners.spotlight: Well drawn, but out of the frame in all six starts and finished tailed off on this month's Bath return (5.7f, good); cheekpieces given a go, but best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well drawn but out of the frame in all six starts and tailed off last time; cheekpieces on

runners.name: In Denial
runners.spotlight: Was making her turf debut when beaten just over a length into third of 15 behind Tickettothestars at Nottingham (6f, good) last month; now 4lb better off and should make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Just over a length behind Tickettothestars at Nottingham last month and 4lb better off

runners.name: Miss Magic Dragon
runners.spotlight: Made it fifth-time lucky at Bath (5.7f, good) this month and ran well under her penalty when beaten just over a length into fourth of 14 at Redcar (6f, good) three days later; not the best of draws, but 1lb lower and shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Won at Bath this month and came fourth at Redcar three days later; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Caitlin G
runners.spotlight: Has failed to beat a rival in all four starts, including on stable/handicap debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) this month; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Has failed to beat a rival in all four starts; look elsewhere

runners.name: Wadirumm
runners.spotlight: Finished around 5l behind the front pair when third of 15 on her handicap/turf debut at Salisbury (6f, good) 12 days ago but that was still a good effort and this easier track may help; high on list on only her fifth outing.
runners.comment: Third on turf debut at Salisbury 12 days ago and this easier track may help; high on list

runners.name: Blossom In The Air
runners.spotlight: Showed little in three starts over 5f-7f on turf/AW for Dominic Ffrench Davis last year; makes her stable/handicap debut (same owner) in first-time cheekpieces after 167 days off; much more needed.
runners.comment: Makes her stable/handicap debut after 167 days off; cheekpieces on; much more needed

runners.name: Jooliah
runners.spotlight: No impact in four starts over 6f/7f on turf/AW last year; big improvement required back from seven months off.
runners.comment: No impact in four starts last year; big improvement required back from seven months off

races.race_name: Greenaway Foundation Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Weffaag
runners.spotlight: 420,000gns yearling; no show in one start at two and although beaten at odds-on when third of 14 on last month's Thirsk reappearance (7f, good), that was still a solid effort; shaped then as though a bit further would suit and a major player with a tongue-tie applied.
runners.comment: Shaped as though further would suit when third over 7f on his reappearance; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Storming Point
runners.spotlight: Sent off 15-2 when beaten a short head on his Newbury debut (6.5f, soft) in October; the ground here is likely to be different, but that was still a promising start; respected.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head on Newbury debut in October; different ground here but still respected

runners.name: Cosmic Girl
runners.spotlight: 72,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Wanees (7f/1m including 2yo/UAE; RPR 103), Condor Pasa (1m1f; 91), Wonder Star (1m2f; 88) and Waldlowe (1m AW; 86); dam 7f/1m winner (including AW/Listed; 98), half-sister to St Leger winner Masked Marvel; hooded on debut, but still enough in her pedigree to make her worth a market check.
runners.comment: Hooded on debut, but still enough in her pedigree to make her worth a market check

runners.name: Ascending Star
runners.spotlight: Third in two starts over 6f at Chester (good to soft) and Hamilton (good) for James Owen last summer; makes his stable debut (same owners) after 31 days off having been gelded in the meantime; market helpful, but is a contender on his debut form in any case.
runners.comment: Third in both starts for another yard last summer; market helpful on stable debut/return

runners.name: Cormorant Rock
runners.spotlight: 300-1 when a well-beaten last of 12 on his debut on the AW here (1m) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Well-beaten last of 12 on his debut on the AW here (1m) a fortnight ago

runners.name: Blazing Sam
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and no immediate promise in two starts at Leicester (6f/7f) this spring.
runners.comment: No immediate promise in two starts at Leicester this spring

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Eazy On The Eye
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) and was demoted after finishing first past the post there in March; 0-8 on turf, though, and this may be an insufficient test for her.
runners.comment: Both wins on the AW and 0-8 on turf; this looks an insufficient test for her

runners.name: Welljudged
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of her six starts on the AW, but well held in both handicaps; bit to prove now switched to turf.
runners.comment: Placed in four of six starts on AW, but well held in both handicaps; bit to prove on turf

runners.name: Daisy Roots
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last summer and has run well on the AW here in her last three outings; still 3lb above her highest winning mark, but ought to put up another bold show.
runners.comment: C&D winner last summer; still 3lb above her highest winning mark, but should still go well

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: Has winning form on turf, but has exclusively raced on the AW since last August, winning twice; favourably treated on the pick of her form on grass and holds an each-way shout.
runners.comment: Favourably treated on the pick of her turf form and holds an each-way shout

runners.name: Land Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in her last ten starts, but is 4lb lower than when making all over C&D this time last year; by no means written off.
runners.comment: Not shown much for a while, but 4lb lower than when winning over C&D this time last year

runners.name: Hilltop
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (good) last August and has won twice on the AW since, the latest in a small field at Newcastle (7f) in March; 76-day break since then not a problem having won twice after similar absences; likely to go well.
runners.comment: C&D winner last August and twice successful on the AW since; likely to go well

runners.name: Summer Heat
runners.spotlight: 0-9; has made the frame eight times over 7f-1m2f, but her mark remains stubbornly static as a result; tongue-tie now given a go, but not sure a sharp 7f like this is ideal.
runners.comment: 0-9, but in the frame eight times; not sure a sharp 7f like this is ideal; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Thunderous Love
runners.spotlight: Winner of five 6f handicaps on turf/AW last year; latest performance was a bit better than it looked and she is now back to her last winning mark, but this trip does appear to stretch her.
runners.comment: Won five times last year and back off last winning mark, but this trip stretches her

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Lohoobb
runners.spotlight: Twice showed ability over 6f last summer but, having been sent off favourite, he didn't get home when fifth of 11 on handicap/seasonal debut at Newmarket (7f. good to firm) this month; a couple of winners have come out of that race and he's a major player down in grade if the run has brought him on.
runners.comment: May have needed his Newmarket reappearance this month; major player down in grade

runners.name: Venetian Romance
runners.spotlight: Not without ability as she showed when fourth of 12 at Leicester (7f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) last time; each-way claims if building on that.
runners.comment: Fourth of 12 at Leicester last time; each-way claims if building on that

runners.name: Luminare
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent, but won at Bath (5f, good to firm) and Newcastle (7f, AW) last year and finished second of six on the AW here (1m) in January; off four months since, but still a player if producing her best form.
runners.comment: Not the most consistent and off four months, but still a player if producing her best form

runners.name: High Approval
runners.spotlight: 400,000euros breeze-up purchase who is now 0-7; not beaten far on his Newmarket return (7f, good) last month and had excuses at Kempton (1m, AW) last time; blinkers now given a go, but he does need to find a bit more.
runners.comment: 400,000euros breeze-up purchase who is now 0-7; does need to find a bit more; blinkers on

runners.name: Mighty Vega
runners.spotlight: Being kept busy having raced 11 times on the AW since November, winning two; is a reliable type, but anyone's guess as to how he will cope with the return to turf; a player if he handles it.
runners.comment: Being kept busy on the AW, but anyone's guess how he will cope with the return to turf

runners.name: Victor Cee
runners.spotlight: Won a four-runner Hamilton maiden (6f, good) for Karl Burke last August and placed in three of his five starts for this yard, but below his best at Newmarket (6f, good) 11 days ago; needs to bounce back on his first attempt at the trip.
runners.comment: Below his best at Newmarket 11 days ago; needs to bounce back on first attempt at the trip

runners.name: Torbados
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in five completions last year, but having been gelded he ran a shocker on last month's Doncaster return (7f, good to firm); blinkers are given a go, but he still has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Ran a shocker on last month's Doncaster return; has plenty to prove; blinkers on

runners.name: Kimbara
runners.spotlight: Best of three efforts this spring when fourth of eight in a Newmarket maiden (7f, good) on his penultimate outing; could take a step forward now switched to a handicap.
runners.comment: Fourth in a Newmarket maiden on penultimate outing; could step forward now handicapping

runners.name: El Potente
runners.spotlight: Produced a good effort in the Frank E. Kilroe Mile to finish runner-up when having two of these behind in March; has a C&D record of four wins from six starts.
runners.comment: Second in the Frank E. Kilroe Mile in March; goes very well here with four C&D wins to his name.

runners.name: Mi Hermano Ramon
runners.spotlight: Finished last of six 52 days ago in an allowance optional claimer; career wins-runs record of 4-16; ought to give it a shot as has been on the scene in the recent past and was placed in the Shoemaker Mile.
runners.comment: Shown signs of promise here and there and wouldn't rule out a big run.
runners.owner: Red Baron's Barn LLC & Rancho Temescal LLC

runners.name: Almendares
runners.spotlight: Successful six times from 23 career runs; landed a narrow success in an allowance Grade 3 last month when four of these were in arrears.
runners.comment: Accounted for several of these last month; admirable career strike-rate (six from 23 to date).
runners.owner: CYBT, McLean Racing Stables Et Al

runners.name: Formidable Man
runners.spotlight: Good effort in the Breeders' Cup Mile when 1 1/2l second at Del Mar in October; has managed eight wins from 16 career runs and a winner of a Grade 2 in July; returns from an absence here; competitive shout as jockey does well from inner gates here.
runners.comment: Can give it a good shot as his jockey has an impressive record from low draws over this C&D.
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs William K Warren Jr

runners.name: King Of Gosford
runners.spotlight: Impressive record here of five wins from eight runs; fourth, beaten just under 2l by Almendares last month in an allowance Grade 3; should figure.
runners.comment: Winner of multiple big races, most recently the Shoemaker Mile so by no means ruled out.
runners.owner: Benowitz Family Trust, CYBT Et Al

runners.name: Genius Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Achieved three wins at this venue and running consistently; missed out by a head behind Almendares 37 days ago upped to this distance in an allowance Grade 3.
runners.comment: Just touched off 37 days ago; producing consistent form.
runners.owner: Pappas Horse Racing Corp, Colin Gala Et Al

runners.name: Seal Team
runners.spotlight: Not had much racing for his age and a proven winner with a career wins-runs record of 3-12; third behind Almendares in an allowance Grade 3 37 days ago upped to this trip.
runners.comment: Not beaten far last month; not had much racing for his age.
runners.owner: Perry R Bass II & Ramona S Bass

runners.name: Call Me Cory
runners.spotlight: Came out on top by 1 1/2l here over 1m1f (allowance optional claimer), showing early speed to lead from a wide draw last month; has won two of his four career runs and going for a hat-trick; down in trip here.
runners.comment: 1 1/2l winner 36 days ago; winner of two of his four runs to date.

runners.name: Twirling Point
runners.spotlight: Creditable effort to finish sixth, beaten just under 3l when behind the reopposing Almendares on debut for this trainer last month in an allowance Grade 3; has managed four wins from 17 career runs.
runners.comment: Far from disgraced last month; career record shows his ability to win (wins-runs record of 4-17).
runners.owner: Tracy D Henline & Paula P Henline
